artists.name: Bushbaby
artists.description:
Since emerging in 2015, Brighton-based DJ and producer Bushbaby has become a driving force in the UK’s new wave of club music, fusing UKG, house and raw rave energy. Known for innovative productions and high-energy sets, he has earned support from Interplanetary Criminal, Chris Stussy, Joy Orbison and BBC Radio 1, as well as co-signs from Four Tet, Disclosure, Jaguar and Sammy Virji.

In 2025 he unveils ‘EVERY TIME’, a 10-track mixtape named after his signature vocal tag, arriving 12 September on Southpoint. The project spans 137–145 BPM and showcases the full scope of his sound, from gritty speed-garage pressure to widescreen, emotional club moments. It is pressed to vinyl and powered by a 40+ date world tour across the UK, Europe, USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand and Japan.

The mixtape caps a landmark run of singles. ‘Shake & Slide’ ignited on socials, ‘Feel The Rush’ revealed his melodic instincts, and ‘Pumpin Jumpin’ has become his biggest record to date, climbing radio and streaming charts with support from MistaJam on Capital Dance and Danny Howard on BBC Radio 1, and heavy club rotation from Skream, Chris Stussy and Riordan.

On the live side Bushbaby’s sets are built for the dancefloor, with 2025 touring in full flow alongside the mixtape rollout. With ‘EVERY TIME’ he distils the last decade of craft into a singular statement and steps into his boldest chapter yet.

artists.name: Sabre
artists.description:
Hailing from North London, 30 year old DJ turned Producer Gove Kidao aka Sabre has risen through the ranks of the global electronic music fraternity since his emergence back in 2002; Securing as he goes a reputation as a twisted yet soulful thinker, bringing a grittier sound to the deeper spectrum of Drum & Bass and experimental electronica.

After having spent his earliest years in the Seychelles, Sabre moved to the UK in 1989. Awed by the richness of the early 90’s UK Music scene; he initially started off as Hip-Hop DJ, switching focus in 1995 to the exciting homegrown movement that was Jungle Drum & Bass.

In the years since those first forays into the scene, Sabre has grown to emerge as a key member of a new generation of Drum and Bass producers; set on evolving the scope of the genre – In the process collaborating with artists such as Noisia, Goldie, Alix Perez, Jenna G, Breakage, Icicle, Sam Frank, Rockwell & Ulterior Motive – but to name a few.

Spanning many styles, this initially dancefloor-orientated catalog has evolved to include a more experimental outlook, showcased in his 2010 debut LP, but perhaps best represented in his contribution towards the score for the feature film “Black water Transit” – Directed by Tony Kaye of “American History X” fame.

Currently resident in Thailands’ bustling capital Bangkok, Sabre continues to produce from his base in this Asian mega-metropolis, returning to Europe throughout the year to tour his residencies and collaborate with his fellow Ivy Lab members.

artists.name: Craze
artists.description:
If the first thing you know about Craze is that he's the only solo DJ to win the DMC World Championships 3 times over, prepare to get yourself schooled. From day one, every uphill battle that Craze faced was met with intense adversity; every kick while he was down only served to help him rise up even stronger. You want proof? Fine.

His family fled Nicaragua for the US when he was 3 in the midst of a civil war and suffered through Hurricane Andrew in '92. Craze was a total nerd through his teenage years. But while others would chalk this up to a lifetime of hurt, Craze let it fuels his passion. By the age of 20, Craze had won his first DMC World Championship. Two more consecutive wins later, he was (and remains to this day) the only three-time winner of the world's most respected international DJ competition, the DMC World Championship - earning the attention of Time Magazine who proclaimed him "America's Best DJ." From Jamaica to Australia, Japan to Nicaragua, Craze has toured the world over, stunning audiences and DJs alike with his top-notch showmanship and masterfully genre-bending sets.

One of those worldwide treks happened to be Kanye West's 2008 Glow In The Dark Tour, where Craze accompanied West as his stage DJ across the globe. Always evolving, Craze founded Slow Roast Records with Kill The Noise, an imprint of the infamous Fool's Gold Records (A-Trak, Kid Cudi, Danny Brown) in 2010. With two releases in 2012 - the Selekta EP and Deeper with Codes, he didn't slow down and instead in April of 2015, released a 4 track EP with the title track called "Baby Gurl" featuring a young superstar on the rise named Troyboi. The EP also boasted a bonus track with Miami rap legend Trick Daddy called "Bow Down". As if that wasn't enough it was also the year, that he brought us one of the most prolific DJ routine videos to date: "New Slaves".

With greatness at the forefront of all Craze does, in 2016 he started a DJ super group with Four Color Zack called 2¢ with the primary goal of creating and presenting the most exciting DJ show on the planet. Since then they have been taking their brand of witty wordplay and impeccable DJ skills to the masses through their mixtapes and live shows which can be summarized as a true tour de force.

This year has already seen the release of his EP entitled K.O.S. Knowledge of Self on Slow Roast Records and his Master Class routine video for Serato, "I Got The Juice" where he revealed not just his DJ skills, but his comedic chops, which begs the question, is there anything Craze can't do? Always humble and putting the performance above all else, Craze has earned his place as an international ambassador of the art of real djing. So when someone asks you "Whatchu know about DJ Craze?", feel free to let them know a thing or two.

artists.name: Hypho
artists.description: Hypho is a British producer, DJ, and founder of Manuka Records, a cutting-edge label launched in 2018. Known for blending dubstep, UKG, breakbeat, and techno, his sound fuses dark textures and percussive energy into a genre-bending signature. With releases on Swamp81, Deep, Dark & Dangerous, Deadbeats, and SubCarbon, his music has earned global acclaim and support from Skream, Truth, and Loefah. Named “One to Watch” by UKF, Hypho continues to push bass culture forward through his label, touring, Rinse FM monthly show, and relentless commitment to innovation.

artists.name: Dane
artists.description: A DJ and seasoned digger of over 20 years, Dane has been fortunate enough to spread his passion for music across countries and continents. He is a Vancouver resident and the host of Dane's Dance Emporium.

artists.name: KiNK
artists.description:
KiNK has become one of house music’s most in demand producers and remixers: his tracks easily stand out in any club night and they are second to none in the most competitive environment electronic music has ever seen. His abilities in finding the ‘sweet spot’ of new machines and even of sounds considered ‘classic’ and to reshape them into totally different beasts would be totally enough to justify the phenomenon. Yet this barely touches the surface of the story.

Arguably the world of dance music has not witnessed anyone else rising so clearly from bottom to top just by the centrifugal force of sheer talent. Being based in Sofia, Bulgaria, without a support network, a campaign or any media hype, his music alone – live and in the studio – created a momentum whose end we have yet to see.

Having met Neville Watson over the internet, he first gained recognition with the duo’s collaborative releases on Amsterdam’s Rush Hour. “Inside Out” and “Full Flight” were huge records in 2008 already, but it was an unassuming video of KiNK tweaking a drum machine at home that landed him a gig at Berlin’s ever emblematic Panorama Bar. By today, he has played the world several times over.

Thus, KiNK’s live sets hold the key to understanding how he creates unparalleled enthusiasm on stage and through his studio output. By now, a guy with a laptop has become the most common sight in a club. Yet the visual discrepancy between the dynamics of sound and the statics of a mouse click has driven most ambitious live acts to sideshow tricks: visuals, LED screens, compensatory dancing – smoke and mirrors. By contrast, KiNK’s live sets simply take the hood off – strictly in terms of sound. A small set up of machines and controllers is all there is, but every knob turned produces an audible result. Often he will pass a machine to the audience and incorporate their input in real time. This happens at mind-boggling speed. Hyperactive, yet holding down grooves to kill for. KiNK’s ability to nail smash beats on the fly, well visibly and audibly, that most people couldn’t program in weeks of studio work – this ‘credibility of sound’ right where it matters is what sparks the love everywhere he visits.

artists.name: Mike Servito
artists.description:
Some DJs burst upon the scene like a supernova, fading just as quickly. Others take years, even decades, to make their way—but those are the ones who make the most impact, thanks to a lifetime of accumulated musical wisdom. Mike Servito is a prime example of the latter. His career is one that’s defined by a slow and steady rise to upper ranks of the deejaying realm—and along the way, he’s perfected a jacking style that equal parts house, acid and techno, a sound that pays homage to the past while being very much of-the-moment.

A Detroit-area native, Servito got his early education by tuning into the Electrifying Mojo and the Wizard (Jeff Mill’s radio moniker). By the early ’90s, Servito was primed to try his hand at the deejaying arts, “I was just kind of cocky about it,” he says. “I knew I could do it.” And he was right—in the DJ hotbed of Detroit, internalizing knowledge gleamed from the likes of Mike Huckaby and Claude Young, he kept working at it until he established himself as a force to be reckoned with in the Motor City circuit, eventually becoming a member in good standing with the inner circle of Ghostly International.

But Servito felt the need for a change from the sometimes insular environment of Detroit. By the late ’00s, he was living in NYC with no real game plan in place. As it turned out, he didn’t really need one—he soon fell in with the city’s premier techno party, The Bunker, becoming one of its mainstay residents. Around the same time, he was beginning to make his presence known on a global scale, packing dance floors with gigs around the States and around the world. But it was a hometown festival snub in 2014 that really got things going.

Servito had played Movement once before, in 2011. “And between then and 2014,” he recalls, “I had been playing so many Bunker parties, playing so many great gigs and my profile was rising—so I was sure they would book me again.” The invite never came. Instead, he booked himself at every Movement after-party he could, one of which was the annual No Way Back, a legendary throw down hosted by The Bunker New York and Detroit’s Interdimensional Transmissions label. The set, a riotously sweaty bleeps-and-beats affair, was recorded for posterity; unexpectedly but deservedly, it garnered a nod as the year’s No 1 online Mix of the Year on Resident Advisor.

That was the spark that set Servito’s career aflame, the push that took him to the top. Since then, he’s cemented his status as one of the most respected spinners around, regularly manning the decks at such hallowed halls as Berlin’s Panorama Bar, London’s XOYO and Tokyo’s Contact, not to mention the world’s best festivals—including, of course Movement. And to this day, his approach to deejaying is refreshingly simple: “I really don’t think about it,” he says, “When I’m playing, I just dive in, and I’m OK.” He’s far more than okay—he’s one of the best around.

artists.name: Cinthie
artists.description:
German producer, DJ, label and record store owner Cinthie has become one of the most sought-after artists in the contemporary electronic scene. Dubbed by many as the Berlin Queen of House, the last decade has seen her touring across the globe to perform at leading clubs and festivals across Europe, Asia, the Americas and beyond.

Cinthie's knowledge of house music's foundations is second to none, as reflected by the vast vinyl collection she has been building since 1995. Her DJ sets cleverly join the dots between the past and the present and plot lines between the swing of New York, the rawness of Chicago and the energy of Berlin.

For more than 20 years her timeless house sounds have come on labels like Heist, Aus Music and her own 803 Crystal Grooves, and in 2020 gave rise to her adventurous and accomplished debut album Skylines - City Lights. Cinthie's DJ-kicks mix in 2022 was another high-water mark.

House music isn't known for its live shows, and even less so for female live artists. But as always, Cinthie is breaking new ground and has recently taken her machines on the road to cult events like Sonar and Mutek. Her influential Elevate. Berlin store is also known for its exceptional taste: every record is handpicked by Cinthie.

artists.name: Lauren Flax
artists.description: As a Detroit native, Lauren Flax was immersed in the freedom and sonic tapestry of the 90s Midwest rave era. She relocated to Brooklyn years ago, but still brings that Midwest energy and diversity to her DJ and live sets, fusing cutting acid lines with the bounce of Chicago jack and Detroit grooves. An in-demand DJ and producer, Lauren’s extensive and unique discography includes remixes and collaborations with a long list of world-class talent like Grammy-nominated artist Sia, Little Boots and Tricky, and a worldwide tour as a member of the successful trip-hop project CREEP. Her releases on The Bunker NY, Truncate’s WRKTRX, a split EP with Posthuman, and  The Sweat EP on Super Rhythm Trax showcase her prowess with techno, house, acid, and electro, while her Out of Reality EP on 2MR dives into more melodic, emotive explorations.

artists.name: Addison Groove
artists.description:
Addison Groove combines all the right flavours of Techno, Electro, Jungle & Juke, stripped down drum beats and acid basslines to carve a unique and infectious groove, giving a unique and versatile approach to many genres of underground music.

It's a rare feat for an artist to garner respect in one genre, let alone across most of underground electronic music. Bristol-based producer Tony Williams AKA Addison Groove (formerly known as Headhunter), managed to do so. A Dubstep mainstay active since 2006, he released a multitude of singles for Tempa as well as an album (Nomad) for the label in 2008.

2010 marked his first release ("Footcrab / Dumbsh*t") as Addison Groove on Loefah's label Swamp 81 and features chopped up samples influenced by the Chicago Juke/Footwork sound. As cited on The Quietus, he played a significant role in introducing this music scene to the U.K. Bass Music continuum and helped solidify the connection between the two, sealing the deal by collaborating with genre pioneer Dj Rashad, featuring on his album "Double Cup" (Hyperdub). He has also released on Pinch's label Tectonic, 50 Weapons (Modeselektor) and 3024 (Martyn).

Since 2012 Addison Groove has toured nightclubs, festivals and broadcasters such as Boiler Room, Fabric (London), Berghain (Berlin), Keep Hush and Glastonbury. Besides Dubstep, his exploration of the UK Bass scene allowed him to gain interest from mainstream media platforms such as BBC Radio 1, Dazed, Vice Magazine and The Red Bull Music Academy where he was a lecturer. He has also collaborated with Sam Binga and Drum & Bass veteran DJ Die and released on Pretty Weird, Exit, Worst Behaviour, Hessle Audio, Gutterfunk and Dance Trax.

artists.name: Coffintexts
artists.description: Part Miami Bass, vintage house bounce & tribal euphoria, Coffintexts' music is a cocktail of indulgence served straight from the queer underground. When she burst onto the scene in the late '10s, she emerged as part of the city's new guard. Equally influenced by everything from 90s tribal rhythms to Latin American-inflected Club to UK rave. Putting out records on labels including Omnidisc, TraTraTrax, Club Romantico, Club QU, Classico & a forthcoming EP on MARICAS, she has kept dance floors bumping from Miami to Shanghai to Bristol, from Nowadays to III Points Festival to HORST Festival.

artists.name: Kahn
artists.description:
Originally honing his craft and making his name as a DJ and producer within Bristol’s early Dubstep scene, Kahn has since gone on to forge his own path within, and leave his mark upon, the world of sound system music. Alongside his musical partner Neek, the duo found themselves at the forefront of Dubstep’s new wave of artists throughout the 2010s, as well as being an instrumental part of the Grime revival of the same period. 

In 2012 they started their own record label, Bandulu Records, as a means of self-releasing their music and giving a platform to other artists from within their community. Combined with a string of releases and collaborations with staple record labels within the scene such as Deep Medi, Keysound Recordings, Butterz and Terrorhythm; Kahn & Neek became synonymous with a style that incorporated the raw, darker side of Dubstep and Grime music with ranging influences from Dancehall to Goth and Punk. 

In 2016 the duo would be invited to curate the 90th edition of the esteemed Fabriclive series, before then featuring on the iconic Essential Mix programme on BBC Radio 1 the following year. 

Following a slew of solo and collaborative releases including remixes for internationally renowned record labels such as Warp Records, False Idols, Exit Records and One Little Indian; 2023 saw the release of Lupus et Ursus, the debut Kahn & Neek full length album project that introduced a whole new breadth of range to their music whilst remaining true to their foundational influences and featuring key collaborators from their journey so far.

With over 80 records under his belt and having toured around the world extensively throughout the last decade, Kahn has launched into the 2020s full of experience and renewed purpose. Continuing to explore and innovate with a signature sound steeped in Bristol’s rich musical heritage and sound system culture.

artists.name: Dance in Connection
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Level up your connection on the dance floor! This hands-on, beginner-friendly workshop dives into the fundamentals of Contact Improvisation and Fusion Partner Dance while integrating consent-based communication techniques. Contact improv is based on physical principles such as weight sharing and momentum; balancing deep listening, collaboration, and pure spontaneity. Fusion draws inspiration from social dance styles like blues, tango, and swing, but with a modern twist: non-gendered leading and following (and switching!), total flexibility, and creative freedom. Together, these forms offer a way to explore the relationship between two people in movement and to navigate the deep currents between us. Come alone, bring a dance partner, or roll with your whole crew! Let’s find the shared pulse that allows us to move, and be moved by one another.

Facilitator Biography
Lo (Loretta Laurin, they/she) is a lover of movements and thrives in the juicy space where somatics meets social justice. With 17 years of teaching experience, they are trained in a range of embodiment modalities, including dance, yoga, pilates, and fitness. Lo's teaching style strikes a unique balance of grounded depth and light-hearted play. They are also a Registered Clinical Counsellor specialising in relationships, personal growth, and trauma. Based in the unceded territories of the Squamish, Musqueam, and Tsleil-Waututh First Nations (Vancouver, BC), they offer in-person and online classes, workshops, and retreats, as well as individual and couples therapy. For more info check out lorettalaurin.com

artists.name: Christian Löffler
artists.description:
Christian Löffler is a German musician, DJ and producer known for his distinctive blend of ambient, techno and house. Born in 1985 near the Baltic Sea, his music reflects a deep connection to nature through emotive, atmospheric soundscapes. Starting with guitar and drums before discovering electronic production in his teens, he quickly shaped a signature style influenced by artists like Paul Kalkbrenner.

His debut album A Forest (2012) introduced his melancholic, minimalist approach and earned widespread praise. Albums such as Mare (2016), Graal (Prologue) (2019) and Parallels (2021, with Deutsche Grammophon) expanded his sound, pairing electronic production with acoustic textures and classical reinterpretations. Löffler is also a visual artist, often incorporating his artwork into his releases.

A captivating live performer, he has toured major venues and festivals worldwide. As co-founder of Ki Records, he supports emerging artists while continuing to evolve his own work. In 2024, he released A Life, further cementing his position as a leading figure in atmospheric electronic music.

artists.name: Opiuo
artists.description:
Opiuo is a genre-bending plethora of auditory and visual masterclasses. Born in 1984 in rural New Zealand, his parents provided the land upon which music festivals were held. This extremely formative growth surrounded by the early incarnations of Aotearoa’s electronic music scene exposed him to the celebration of life through self-expression, dance, and giant sound systems. These experiences shaped so much of who he is & what he stands for today. Positivity and movement at the core of the project, his music is an infectious electronic based blend of funk, soul, psychedelia, and booty-shaking bass.

Now calling Australia home, Oscar tours his party-starting music throughout the globe. Having spent more than a decade performing at some of the world's most renowned festivals and venues including Lollapalooza, Glastonbury, and Coachella, as well as selling out Colorado’s famous Red Rocks Amphitheatre multiple times, he’s performed his music in an unimaginably large array of formats. From self-built Orchestras, to collaborating with symphony choirs, touring his own Opiuo Band, and as an immersive solo audio & visual show, his live creativity is limitless.

Taking home the New Zealand Music Award for Best Electronic Album in 2014, his catalogue is vast. Having released 6 full-length albums, 10 EPs, and a myriad of singles and remixes over the last 14 years. In his own words, he is still "just getting started!"

artists.name: Daphni
artists.description: In 2011, Dan Snaith aka Caribou, looking for an outlet for more dancefloor influenced output, began releasing music under the name Daphni. He has released 3 studio albums under this alias – Jiaolong (2012), Joli Mai (2017) and Cherry (2022) on his own record label, Jiaolong. When appearing as Daphni, Snaith performs as a DJ, having played repeatedly at clubs, including Panorama Bar, Printworks, Fabric, Plastic People, Space Ibiza, Trouw, Nitsa, Nowadays, and festivals, including Coachella, Glastonbury, Dekmantel, Primavera Sound, Field Day, Melt, Rainbow Disco Club, Junction 2, amongst many others.

artists.name: Paul Woolford
artists.description:
"The essence of rave comes alive in Paul Woolford's music, whether he tackles thundering breakbeats, ecstatic piano house, or spring-loaded techno, with a prolific stream of ebullient, floor-filling house" - Apple Music.

Originally from Leeds, Paul Woolford began going to raves during the early 1990s, and eventually became a resident DJ at Back to Basics, a legendary techno night that took place in his home city. He gradually taught himself how to produce tracks, and by the end of the decade, he began releasing tech-house records with Tony Senghore, eventually calling themselves The Wackdaddies. In 2000, Woolford signed to 20:20 Vision as Bobby Peru and began releasing house records infused with funk and electro, making his full-length solo debut with 2002's Death of a Player. He additionally released material under short-lived monikers like Hip Therapist and Skip Donahue, and first used his own name on the Subliminal-issued 2002 release “Without You/Only the Wild.” His mid-2000s output, including records titled “Jack Factory” and “The Jackford Files,” paid homage to classic Chicago house, while the three-part Modernist EP series in 2006 was inspired by Detroit techno.

The Bobby Peru single "Erotic Discourse" was also released in 2006 and became one of his most widely played tracks. In the same year he won the award for “Best Newcomer” in DJ Mag’s lauded end-of-year awards. Woolford launched his Intimacy label in 2007, with the EP Body Double, and his second Bobby Peru album, The Truth, arrived in 2008. In connection with his popular weekly residency at Ibiza's Space club, he mixed the first disc of the Ministry of Sound-issued We Love Space 08. Another mix, Renaissance: Platform, appeared in 2010, the same year he was voted “Best DJ” in DJ Mag’s Best of British Awards. Woolford collaborated with Psycatront on that year's Cocoon-issued "Thunder" single, followed by 2011's "Stolen," his first release for U.K. bass label Hotflush. He also released well-received records on Planet E ("Achilles") and Phonica ("Can't Do Without"), and reunited with Mat Playford (whom he previously worked with as Snow Monkeys) for a single as Ford Inc, issued by Throne of Blood.

2012 saw the release of Woolford's ambitious double-CD mix The Lab 04, as well as the "Pursuit" single on Halo Cyan. Woolford also began releasing white label 12"s as Special Request in 2012, taking influence from breakbeat hardcore and jungle. Houndstooth signed the project and issued the full-length Soul Music in 2013; its CD edition came with a bonus disc featuring tracks from the earlier singles and remixes, including his notorious mix of Lana Del Rey 's "Ride." Woolford 's second Hotflush single, "Untitled," became another major club hit, and was picked up by Ultra Records/Sony (with the subtitle "Call Out Your Name") in 2014. Hotflush released a pair of remixes of "Erotic Discourse," followed by the 2015 single "Orbit." As Special Request, Woolford issued the three-part Modern Warfare EP series on XL Recordings.

He continued using the moniker for his more experimental work, including the ambitious, cinematic double album Belief System (2017), as well as his Fabriclive 91 mix. His more club-friendly output was released under his own name, including the piano-heavy "Forevermore" on Running Back, as well as more spiritually inclined material such as the Aus Music-released Father, Son & Holy Ghost and Heaven & Earth. As Special Request kept pushing jungle and breakbeat to the limits with releases like 2019's Vortex and Zero Fucks, Woolford released some of his most successful mainstream club material under his own name and won both DJ Mag’s “Best Producer” and “Best Album” for Vortex.

He worked with Chicago house diva Kim English on the 2018 FFRR single "Hang Up Your Hang Ups" and Newcastle singer/songwriter Karen Harding on "You Already Know," issued by Positiva in 2019. Since then, the output under both his eponymous Paul Woolford and Special Request aliases has become what could easily be described as at breakneck speed. Between 2023 and 2024 he’s had 34 individual releases including originals and remixes with Allison Goldfrapp, Lewis Thompson and MNEK, Sonny Fodera and Ella Henderson, LF System and Shayan, Aluna, and Novelist, as well as the much-revered Portals four-volume series, and the recent STREET ANTHEMS self-released LP which coalesced at the end of last year.

artists.name: Emrrld
artists.description: Emrrld is the expansive musical alias of Tyler Freele. Known equally for his spirited productions and smooth vinyl blends, Emrrld brings a dynamic energy to wherever he plays. From deep listening to lively dance floors, Emrrld’s sound is rooted in a magnetic bass-driven minimalism. Flipping samples and working largely with an ever-changing palette of hardware, his productions bring you to a unique yet nostalgic world. Emrrld’s collection of independent releases and his weighty record crate generate a momentous yet deeply rooted sound.

artists.name: Dizzy
artists.description: Your favourite hypeman is back! Dizzy Dlux has been the voice of the vibe for many stages across the country and beyond. “It feels good to be back, Bass Coast 2026, LET’S GO!” – D!zzy

artists.name: Dylan Fogarty
artists.description:
"Dublin-based techno artist Dylan Fogarty has been at the forefront of his city’s techno revival. Embracing the playful, loop-driven approach of ‘00s techno while weaving in elements of house, garage, and dub. His sound, a rich tapestry of groove, swing, and unadulterated funk, channels the golden era of techno, striking a balance between Stockholm and Napoli while shaping the next generation of the genre in Ireland and beyond.

With releases on Molekül, Frenzy, and RAW, as well as a string of highly regarded self-released projects, aptly titled The Forgotten Loops, D Loops, 16 Bar Shuffle Tools, and more, Fogarty's intent is clearly rooted in the essence of classic techno. His productions are oozing with character and swagger, making him a key figure in the movement back towards the raw energy of the ‘00s loop-driven club sounds.

Despite his growing status, Fogarty remains somewhat of an enigma; he has never given an interview, rarely posts on social media, and often drops new music spontaneously via Bandcamp, embodying the DIY ethos of the ‘90s in both sound and spirit.

With an encyclopedic knowledge of techno, house, and garage that spans decades, Fogarty’s artistry runs far deeper than trends. While much about him remains elusive, one thing is certain: when he plays, standing still is simply not an option."

artists.name: DJ dood
artists.description:
DJ dood is a Vancouver-based DJ and promoter known for high-energy, groove-driven sets. Moving fluidly between house, tech house and techno, she pairs cheeky vocals with rolling basslines, balancing a love for classic dancefloor moments with a playful, forward-looking approach.

A regular presence in the city’s underground, she has shared bills with artists including Moxie, Cinthie, DJ Boring, Prosumer and Brawther, and made her Bass Coast debut in 2024. She also runs two party series: dood’s dancefloor, spotlighting queer and femme talent, and Full House, a collaboration with Max Ulis (SLAB) bringing together local and international artists.

Beyond the booth, she works as Programming Manager for Public Disco, helping transform unexpected urban spaces into open air dancefloors. Her footprint across Vancouver’s scene includes appearances at venues like Industrial, Platform 9, Fortress, Village Studios, Celebrities and The Birdhouse.

artists.name: Fort Knox Five
artists.description:
Fort Knox Five originated in Washington, D.C., conceived as a dynamic project dedicated to the exploration of all things funky, transcending the boundaries of genre and tempo. From its inception, the essence was steeped in retro vibes, the sound pulsating with funk, and the beats resonating with freshness. Now, two decades into the journey, FK5 stands resilient, an enduring force in the musical landscape.

Melding live instrumentation with electronic prowess, Fort Knox Five crafts seamless grooves that traverse the spectrum from funk to house, reggae to glitch hop, and culminate in prime-time bangers. They have been at the forefront of a contemporary funk revolution, embarking on a musical odyssey that has witnessed them sharing stages with industry giants and icons for nearly two decades.

In recent years, Steve Raskin has assumed the role of the principal DJ, steering the Fort Knox brigade towards a funky future. As he now leads the charge, he commands the stage, delivering high-energy sets that showcase their signature blend of glitched-out funk. From renowned clubs to some of the largest festivals worldwide, Raskin electrifies the atmosphere with infectious grooves seamlessly transitioning from funk, bass, and hip hop to prime-time house and breaks, ensuring that the party never falters.

artists.name: Downtown Solutions
artists.description:
Downtown Solutions (Jean Brazeau) is a recording artist, promoter, and DJ from Vancouver, Canada. Working under multiple aliases (F7, ZAP! Deejays, Chapters, and Friendly Chemist), he has released music on the labels Acting Press, Pacific Rhythm, Isla, and 1080p.

A former curator of the Vancouver music venue and project space Deep Blue, Jean has been producing music, playing live, operating studios, booking shows, and designing flyers since 2015. Alongside All Rest No People, he was nominated for a 2026 Juno Award for the single ""Icarus"" from their collaborative album Lost In Flower (Acting Press, 2025).

Since 2022, Jean regularly plays records at ZAP!, a celebrated Vancouver party series he presents alongside Scott W.

artists.name: DJ Minx
artists.description:
Detroit’s DJ Minx, often hailed as the First Lady of Wax, is a pioneering force in house and techno. Since the early 1990s, she has been shaping Detroit’s underground through her Women on Wax collective and label, legendary club residencies, and an unbroken run of performances at the Detroit Electronic Music Festival / Movement since its inception.

Her 2003 track “A Walk in the Park” remains a minimal house classic, later reissued on Richie Hawtin’s M_nus imprint.

Celebrated as one of the women who shaped dance music history by Mixmag and honoured with Detroit’s Spirit of Detroit Award, Minx has recently expanded her global presence with releases on Planet E, HE.SHE.THEY., and Rules Don’t Apply. In 2026, she continues to shine as a proud advocate for Black queer voices, a powerful performer, and a cultural ambassador, keeping Detroit’s spirit alive on dancefloors worldwide.

artists.name: Skratch Bastid
artists.description: Skratch Bastid has been moving dance floors and captivating crowds across the globe for over two decades, establishing himself as one of the world’s most respected and beloved DJs. From  packed clubs and massive stages to his legendary annual Bastid’s BBQs, he brings a lively energy and surgical precision to every set. Known for his electric stage presence, technical prowess and versatile selections that span the complete musical spectrum—from hip hop to house, disco to dancehall, reggae to funk and back again—Bastid is a true crowd favourite with an unrivaled ability to create unforgettable moments with not only music, but the community.

artists.name: Fisher Bryce + educación física
artists.description:
Local tastemakers Fisher Bryce and educación física are co-founders of teXture, a queer party series/collective in Vancouver. 

Together, they blur the lines between proggy-house, techno, trance, electro, and acid. Fisher Bryce’s nuanced, emotionally driven selections meet educación física’s percussive intensity and body-focussed drive, creating a dynamic push-and-pull between introspection and release. 

Despite coming from very different backgrounds, the two are a classic example of how sometimes our differences make us stronger.

artists.name: Special Request
artists.description: UK-born producer and DJ Paul Woolford channels the raw energy of late-’80s pirate radio, jungle, breakbeat and rave under his Special Request alias.

artists.name: DUST and BEAU
artists.description: Sharing the same DNA, twin brothers Dust & Beau move in near-telepathic sync behind the decks, reading each other’s energy and performing as one unified force. Their sets flow effortlessly across genres, guiding listeners through bass-driven landscapes from around the globe. From reimagined classics and edits to their own original productions, one thing is certain—their infectious energy keeps the dance floor moving.

artists.name: Esette & Shael
artists.description:
Esette (Isis Graham) and Shael have been connected through music and friendship for over two decades, coming up together in Western Canada’s underground house scene as women on the front line. From Calgary to Winnipeg and beyond, their paths have moved through festivals, clubs, and community-driven projects, shaping a shared perspective rooted in longevity and stewardship of the culture.

Together, their sets reflect that history. Esette’s deep, groove-focused approach meets Shael’s fluid, mood-driven selections, drawing on influences from 90s house through to present-day sounds. When they play together, there’s a sense of ease and connection that comes from years of shared experience, carried by a genuine love for the music and each other.

artists.name: Foxy Moron
artists.description:
fOxy moron is a Whistler-based DJ specializing in not taking things too seriously and playing delightfully unexpected music. With breaks as the backbone, her sets wander through genres to whatever strange gem feels right in the moment—keeping dancefloors moving, confused, and occasionally laughing. Still rocking turntables and often spinning all-45 vinyl sets, she’s exactly what you need for that slightly dusty, sun-soaked final festival afternoon. When not curating dancefloor drollery, she can often be found pursuing the perfect grilled cheese sandwich.

Written by AI, the official (and slightly suspicious) author of this bio. ????????????

artists.name: Girth Wirkus
artists.description:
Girth Wirkus, founder of Space Daze. Mask wearing, vinyl spinning dubstep DJ.

There is freedom in the constraints of vinyl. It is all about feeling. Sometimes it's not going to work out perfectly. However, the uncertainty and pressure of deciding what to play next often leads to something special. I never plan sets. I like to be loose and live in the moment.

artists.name: DJ Hannah
artists.description: DJ Hannah is a selector, event producer and music enjoyer based out of “Calgary”/Mohkinstsis. Shaped by a lifelong obsession with the discothèque, she is known for her deep-reaching, multi-genre sensibility and steady control behind the decks. Blending house, disco, techno, and left-of-centre selections with ease, she builds sets that feel story-driven and hypnotic. Since her club debut in 2017, she’s become a mainstay in Western Canada’s electronic music scene, with a presence that stretches from club residencies to major festival stages. Whether she’s curating events at Calgary’s Common/UNDRGRD, slinging records at Edmonton’s Mimi Bar or hosting her CJSW radio show, Neon Window, Hannah creates sonic experiences that are both intimate and expansive.

artists.name: DSAV
artists.description: With origins in house music, DSAV has evolved into a versatile force in the bass scene. From 140 dubstep to DnB and UKG, his sets blend originals and influences into a signature vibe recognized as the Danik Bounce, a sound that consistently moves crowds.

artists.name: Welcome Ceremony
artists.description: Elder Richard Jackson, Jr. Vietnam War Veteran

artists.name: DJ_2Button
artists.description: dj_2button is a multidisciplinary artist, DJ and founder of Weirdos Inc. Known for his left-of-centre, eclectic and psychedelic sound, his sets move through dubby, wonky territory — percussive, brash and transcendent, seen through a lens rooted in post-punk and new wave. Constantly searching for the head-turning moment, he aims to shake things in the most unexpected places with an anything-goes mentality.

artists.name: Fizch
artists.description: Colombian-born and Vancouver-based, Fizch is a DJ, producer, and core force behind Vantek Records. With over a decade in the scene, his sound has shifted from raw hard techno to hypnotic groove-driven, focused on tension and flow. He has played alongside artists such as Adriana Lopez, Chris Liebing, and Richie Hawtin. Beyond the booth, he releases on Vantek and Resilient Recordings, while contributing as Label Manager, helping define a distinct underground identity.

artists.name: Dunks
artists.description: Dunks is a versatile DJ, producer, and multimedia artist making a strong mark on the global music scene. As one half of The Funk Hunters and founder of Lazy Syrup Orchestra, he bridges electronic beats, live instrumentation, and visual media. From headlining festivals worldwide to curating heartfelt live shows, he is known for seamless genre blending in long-form DJ sets that create immersive journeys on the dance floor. With millions of streams and a loyal fanbase, Dunks continues to push boundaries while staying rooted in the community.

artists.name: Edgar Cal
artists.description: Rooted in classic house, his sound blends minimal, deep tech, electro, techno, and beyond, delivering powerful, groove-driven sets built for the dancefloor. With over 15 years behind the decks, Edgar Cal has performed at top clubs across Spain and released music on international labels like Moan Rec. and House Salad. Now based in Canada, he is part of Vantek Group’s roster and has played events for leading promoters in BC, including Gorg-O-Mish, Soma Music Hub and Shambhala Music Festival.

artists.name: VNSSA
artists.description:
Global Circuit Queen: VNSSA’s Surreal Sonic Uprising Introducing VNSSA — the California-born artist, producer, and DJ whose raw blend of house, disco, techno, and gritty punk attitude has become a defining force on dance floors worldwide. From her early days drumming in indie bands to djing major festivals, VNSSA has carved out a lane that’s as rebellious as it is refined.

After making a splash with high-impact releases on Dirtybird, Higher Ground, and Sweat It Out, VNSSA continues to evolve her sound while expanding her influence. Her latest run of releases on Three Six Zero Recordings — including Grindhouse and Giving You — have found support from global tastemakers and playlist curators alike, landing placements on Spotify’s Housewerk, Operator, and Dance Party, as well as Apple Music’s One Mix. 2025 has already marked a major career moment: VNSSA’s secret guest appearance at Coachella’s Do LaB stage as part of Sara Landry’s Blood Oath — a revolutionary all-female collective — cemented her place among dance music’s fiercest rising forces. Her collaboration with Walker & Royce on “I Don’t Remember” has dominated clubs, festivals, and airwaves, amplifying her presence across streaming platforms and DJ sets alike. 

When she’s not on stage, VNSSA curates her own monthly show, Super Creep Radio, and Insomniac Radio, where she shines a spotlight on cutting edge underground talent and showcases her ever-evolving taste. This year, VNSSA takes her show global — playing Lightning in a Bottle, Outside Lands, Hard Summer, EDC Las Vegas, and more — bringing her unmistakable hybrid of percussive grit and dancefloor allure to bigger stages than ever before. 

VNSSA is also one half of the newly launched duo Girl Math, alongside rising star Nala. The project merges live vocals, indie dance, and analog hardware with club-ready DJ sets—delivering an unfiltered, high-energy blend of punk femme chaos and indie sleaze revival. Equal parts nostalgic and fresh, Girl Math is redefining what a back-to-back can be. Whether behind the decks, in the studio, or on the road, VNSSA is the moment — and she’s just getting started. “VNSSA does not exist in the world of music because it is her choice, she exists in it because it is her calling.”

artists.name: ADAM & Subdidi
artists.description: Subdidi and ADAM are a duo force hailing from “Vancouver” with formative roots built in Edmonton. A friendship that was born from a love of music has a synergy that can be palpably felt on the decks through to the dancefloor. Operating as both visual and sonic artists, they have a rich backdrop of references, creating sets that are culturally informed and forward-thinking. Their B2Bs are sonic conversations, feeling like cascading moments of serendipity and banter. With tastes spanning the Black hardcore continuum, post-club, hip-hop, PBNJ, gqom and more, they always find a way to land on a central nexus of rhythm, resistance, and back-breaking bass. This Bass Coast, their set will be guaranteed to keep you on the edge of your seat.

artists.name: Civillian
artists.description:
Eclectic is an understatement when trying to describe the sound of Civillian. While always staying true to his roots in Hip Hop, his taste and collection have expanded massively to allow him to become a party-rocking chameleon - no situation outside of his scope. Besides his proven taste and wide range in selection, his ability to work in elements of Turntablism into a dance-floor friendly set is what separates him from other DJs. Whether it’s the main stage, an impromptu sunrise set, a house party, or an underground rave, Civillian is always prepared.

While always striving to entice the dancefloor with sounds both new and familiar, his uncanny versatility, combined with the biggest grin on the West Coast, has truly made Civillian a DJ of the people.

artists.name: Angel Science
artists.description:
Angel Science AKA Diodore is the producer, deejay and curator behind the label ‘Traditional Navigation Systems’. 

Recognized for crafting deeply rhythmic and cathartic sonic journeys, he holds a strong stance in Canada's underground dance music scene. He is internationally known for releasing work on labels such as PHTM, Kluster, Weirdos Inc., Echolocations, Substation, Heistmode, Kudatah and more. 

What to expect? Whether it be an exploration through realms of high intensity textural psychedelia, stripped back chuggers, evolving breaky minimal, temple air ambience, organic basslines and everything in between, this is not an hour to miss.

artists.name: Visages
artists.description:
Founded in 2017, the Toulouse-based collective ‘Visages’ were brought together by several years of friendship and a shared love of event management (Dusk DNB) in their hometown. 

The 4 piece ensemble consists of Valentin, Etienne, Tom and Kévin. Each member brings their own area of expertise and point of view, gelling together perfectly. 

Their musical influences are very diverse, from Hip Hop to Rock, from Reggae to Ambient. This is reflected in their productions with a wide range of diversity, alternating between soulful drum and bass and deep dubstep.

artists.name: The Funk Hunters
artists.description:
Canadian DJ duo The Funk Hunters - Nick Middleton and Duncan Smith - have spent nearly two decades electrifying dancefloors worldwide with their multisensory approach to live music. Performing on four decks and two mixers, their funk-infused sets blend house, bass, midtempo, and drum & bass with custom-synced live visuals - crafting a spontaneous experience designed to move bodies and inspire minds.

With more than 50 million streams, The Funk Hunters’ catalog spans hit collaborations, original mixes, and official remixes for Opiuo, CloZee, Gramatik and global stars including U2 and Imagine Dragons. Their production work has reached #1 on the Billboard Dance Club Chart, while their critically acclaimed album projects have showcased heavyweight collaborators like Dr. Fresch, Flowdan, Big Gigantic, and Chali 2na and Cut Chemist of Jurassic 5 fame - cementing their role as tastemakers and community builders.

On stage, they’ve become fixtures at the world’s most iconic festivals and venues - Red Rocks, Coachella, Shambhala, Bonnaroo, Electric Forest, Outside Lands, Life Is Beautiful, Burning Man - earning a reputation as one of electronic music’s most exciting live acts. From underground roots to international acclaim, The Funk Hunters are pushing electronic music forward with a sound that is as timeless as it is fresh.

artists.name: Andy Warren & Peter Sheppard
artists.description: Andy Warren and Peter Sheppard are Portland-based DJs, producers, and co-founders of Process PDX, with deep roots in the city’s underground dance music scene. The two of them have been DJing together for a decade at their open-air day party, Your Sunday Best. Andy’s sets move through house, techno, disco, and experimental shades, while Peter’s sound is shaped by early influences in jazz and funk. Together, they bring a shared focus on community, collaboration, and building space for dancers through their events and collective work.

artists.name: AWood
artists.description:
Behind AWood’s distinct energy lies their groovy track selection, old school mixing style, and a deep love for electronic music. They’ve brought their sound to festivals like Bass Coast, Shambhala, and Re-Union, and have shared the booth with renowned techno artists in clubs and warehouses from Vancouver to Toronto. In recent years, they have forged a deeper connection to music through playing all vinyl sets. 

Between releases and performances, AWood is committed to giving back, volunteering their time to teach DJing and music production under their Curve YVR project. Inspired by the countless moments they’ve shared on dance floors throughout their life, AWood is thrilled to bring their style back to Basscoast for a late-night set this year.

artists.name: BB Rose
artists.description: BB ROSE is an emerging DJ bringing the pulse of UK garage to Vancouver’s underground dance scene. Blending UKG, infectious 2-step, bassline, and speed garage, her sets are driven by groove, energy, and a deep love for forward-thinking club sounds. BB ROSE has quickly begun making her mark within Vancouver’s nightlife community, earning attention for her sharp selections and ability to keep dance floors moving. Her style pulls from the rich history of UK garage while leaning into the evolving sounds of modern bass music.  Known for her high-energy approach and instinct for reading a crowd, BB ROSE delivers sets that feel both nostalgic and fresh—packed with rolling basslines, crisp percussion, and undeniable momentum. As she continues to build her presence, she’s quickly becoming one of the city’s most exciting emerging selectors in the garage and bass scene.

artists.name: Big Body
artists.description:
Big Body is a longtime fixture in the Victoria dance music scene, both behind and in front of the booth. He's a dancer's DJ, slinging sassy 90s house gems, euphoric prog groovers and bossy club-ready thumpers. Weird, cute, fun. Leaving audiences smiling and sweaty, he digs for the hidden gems of today and yesterday — your new favourite tunes.

Big Body is also the founder of Vibrate, a club night built to explore the dimensions of the broader 4/4 sound. He is also the creator of Voyages.fm, a psychedelic mix series for guided inward exploration.

artists.name: Archivist
artists.description: Archivist is a Calgary-based DJ / producer with a passion for integrating funk/groove-based house with tech & breakbeat elements. A focus on combining dance-forward deep cuts and unconventional soundscapes to create something unique in the overlay. His production style ranges through avenues of deep house, breaks & lo-fi with a distinctive emphasis on spacey synths and layered textures.

artists.name: Coltcuts
artists.description:
Introducing ColtCuts - Voted #1 Best Breakthrough Producer of 2019 in the Duploc Dubstep Awards. 

Colt has quickly become an internationally recognized producer with releases on many of the most prestigious labels in the scene (Deep, Dark & Dangerous / Wheel & Deal / Duploc / UKF & more). With a full release schedule through 2025/2026, this is one producer to keep your ears open for. 

ColtCuts is known for his variety of productions, ranging from Dubstep all the way to modern halftime, giving a wide array of sounds to his live performance. His sound has captured the interest of countless industry heavyweights with a no-nonsense approach, impeccable sound design and tasteful balance of darkness and light.

artists.name: The Librarian
artists.description:
Andrea Graham, known professionally as The Librarian, is a pioneering DJ, producer, label owner, and festival founder who has established herself as one of bass music's most influential creative forces and tastemakers. As the co-founder and music director of Bass Coast Festival – internationally acclaimed for its innovative music programming and immersive installation art – she has built an inclusive electronic music community that resonates with audiences worldwide.

The Librarian's dynamic performances seamlessly traverse the full spectrum of bass music, deftly moving between 130 breaks and adjacent techno to UKG, deep dubstep, jungle, juke and drum and bass. Her sets are characterized by powerful low-end frequencies with nuanced melodic elements and an intuitive understanding of dancefloor dynamics, creating deep connections with audiences across the globe. With a touring career spanning seventeen years playing clubs and festivals, she has refined her craft through countless transformative nights on dance floors worldwide.

Her extensive touring schedule has taken her throughout North America, Australia, Central America, and India, with landmark performances at some of the world's most prestigious venues and festivals. Recent highlights include a compelling set at the iconic Red Rocks Amphitheatre and an electric 360 degree performance at The Caverns in Tennessee. Her festival appearances span Coachella, Electric Forest, Lightning in a Bottle, Beyond Wonderland, Bumbershoot, Rainbow Serpent, Shambhala, and many more. Her legendary Boiler Room and Keep Hush performances are on repeat.
Based in Squamish, BC, Canada, Andrea balances her touring career with her role as Bass Coast Festival's music director while also heading her record label, Chapter Three Music.

The Librarian maintains a consistent presence in the electronic music scene by touring regularly and hosting her monthly radio show, Book Club. She continues to push creative boundaries with her own productions.

Following her acclaimed trilogy of singles, she released a captivating 6-track EP, "miss u," further cementing her reputation as a multifaceted artist who continues to shape the future of bass music.

"A bass music legend" –Billboard
"(The Librarian) is a renaissance woman...a name impossible to ignore in the burgeoning international bass scene." –Earmilk
"The Librarian never disappoints, with her deep love for the art of bass music reflected in her sets" —Billboard

artists.name: Carmen (Live)
artists.description:
Carmen represents a multiverse of soundscapes, a non-pretentious alter ego that has been turning heads for its intrepid live sets and modular synth manipulation. With a profound passion for electronic music, the Portuguese artist/producer has forged a distinct identity within the underground techno community.

Carmen’s career has been marked by performances in some of Portugal's most prestigious clubs & festivals, including the renowned Gare, in Porto, and Vinculum Festival 2025, sharing booths with artists like Dax J, DJ Nobu, Gigi FM, and many more. With a vision for the future that embraces collaboration and innovation, Carmen is poised to leave a lasting mark on the electronic music scene, making waves both locally and internationally.

artists.name: Surusinghe
artists.description:
From DJing, to producing, promoting, labels, and management - there isn’t much that Australian born, London based Surusinghe isn't involved in. Experimenting with bass, reggaeton, broken-beat and techno, Surusinghe keeps her mixes fast and unexpecting. Surusinghe's releases to date have been put out by some of the most essential tastemakers in the game — AD 93, Steel City Dance Discs and TraTraTrax amongst others. She's played b2b with a stellar cast of scene-leaders, from HAAi to Bitter Babe and Moktar. The whiplash momentum of her gig schedule has carried her across continents, from Nowadays in New York to Drumsheds in London, Unsound in Krakow to Boiler Room in Naarm. While her sound on this new EP is tooled for the peak time, it's also subtle and supple enough to look beyond obvious ear candy tropes and delve a little deeper into what a dancefloor actually needs.

Surusinghe's duty of care for the rave manifests in everything she does. She co-founded multifaceted platform Drifting to put on seismic club line-ups and revive music-first blog discourse with heavyweight guests such as Bicep. When she's playing on a multi-room bill, she actively works to offer a musical counterpoint to whatever else is happening in the club. Her determination to school the next generation on the value of going out dancing is a driving force in her trajectory as an artist.

Pursuing her passion with integrity while blazing a searing path right into the heart of contemporary techno, Surusinghe sounds stronger than ever on her DH2 debut, leaving you with a four-strong statement you won't forget in a hurry. You can catch her toasting this significant next step through a busy Spring-Summer schedule that takes in DGTL in Amsterdam, Nowadays' infamous Nonstop session in New York, GALA in Peckham, Outlook Origins in Croatia and Dekmantel in the Netherlands.

artists.name: C-Star
artists.description: C-Star is known for her high-energy performances, which incorporate groovy and acid-laced techno with pounding rhythms, bringing a lively texture to her sets. She currently holds a DJ residency with Vantek Group, as well as Raised by Raves. C-Star has played numerous gigs, festivals, and clubs, gracing the decks all across Canada. She has been booked with well-respected artists such as Richie Hawtin, Mark Broom, Lee Ann Roberts, A*S*Y*S, Adriana Lopez, Regal, Øtta, John 00 Fleming, and many more.

artists.name: Burchill
artists.description:
Burchill is a dynamic force in Western Canada’s electronic music scene, coming off his biggest year yet. His Winter/Spring tour saw him headlining shows across Canada, lighting up dance floors in both cities and mountain towns with his signature blend of genre-bending bass & high-energy grooves. 

Burchill brought that same energy to a packed 2025 festival season, with standout sets at Shambhala, Wicked Woods, Badlands, The Womp, Electric Love, Zen Garden, Pachena Bay, and more. His performances have earned him a loyal regional following and growing recognition for his ability to command crowds with deep musicality and infectious stage presence.

He has released original music on Hysteria Records, WestWood Recordings, Rock Bottom Records and more, to go along with his SoundCloud mixes, receiving hundreds of thousands of plays. This has given him a following that has grown organically, just like his music career. From humble beginnings to seeing his sound travel further and connect with wider audiences, Burchill is a reminder that consistency, passion, and showing up for the scene really do matter.

artists.name: 2flowers
artists.description: 2flowers is a DJ, curator, and music collector who shares music with deep intentionality, creating space to exist as yourself on the dancefloor. Shaped by Chicago-area roots, they move across the spectrum of dance music with an ear for analog grooves and records with more character than formula. Their DJing is a celebration of Blackness, gender euphoria, and a lifelong connection to music. In 2025, they co-created the immediately impactful Vancouver event series Altered Soul with visual artist Clara.

artists.name: Young Franco
artists.description: Young Franco is well and truly establishing himself as one of Australia's most legendary DJ and producers. His debut album ‘it’s Franky baby! (released Jan 2025) reached #1 on the ARIA Top 10 Australian Artists Dance Albums with over 90 million streams to date. His discography is sitting on over 300 million streams across all singles to date and has four ARIA Certified Gold records. The past year has seen him take stages all across the US, Europe, Asia, Australia and New Zealand for a string of sold-out headline shows. Alongside his impressive catalogue, Young Franco has released official remixes for Glass Animals & Denzel Curry’s ‘Tokyo Drifting’, Charli XCX ‘1999’, Dua Lipa’s ‘IDGAF’, Ruel’s ‘Painkiller’ w/ Denzel Curry & Kool & The Gangs 'Get Down On It’.

artists.name: Handsome Tiger
artists.description:
Handsome Tiger, an Anishinaabe Métis and North African music producer/DJ hailing from Turtle Island and currently based in Vancouver, BC, blends traditional sounds from his heritage with other Indigenous and contemporary electronic bass music. A familiar face in the West Coast music community, Handsome Tiger has performed at numerous shows and amassed a collection of releases. With a passion for bass music, he actively supports and contributes to Vancouver's underground scene and has gained popularity in the North American festival circuit, appearing at events such as Bass Coast, Shambhala, Envision, Lightning in a Bottle, Mutek, Texas Eclipse, and more. His DJ sets fuse various flavours of sound system culture and global club sounds, igniting dance floors wherever he performs.

Handsome Tiger has graced the stage alongside a diverse array of artists, including EarthGang, DJ Shub, Snotty Nose Rez Kids, Ky-Mani Marley, Sister Nancy, The Librarian, DJ MADD, TMSV, Madam X, D Double E, Lady Chann, Hypho, Distinct Motive, and Lighta! Sound.

artists.name: Crystal Precious (Host)
artists.description:
Equal parts burlesque showgirl and late-night warehouse MC, Crystal Precious is a multi-hyphenate performer, writer, community organizer, and recording artist.

Her music blends bass-heavy, 90s-inspired atmospheric hip-hop with torchy vocals and a wink of tongue-in-cheek sass.

Over two decades, she’s built platforms for artists across Vancouver, including private venues like Fortress, Crystal Ballroom, and Dollhouse, as well as the Vancouver International Burlesque Festival.

Her interview podcast Backstage Cash is a survivor’s guide to the business of art, while her Substacks—precious-list and Adventures in Sass—offer a glimpse into her world behind the scenes.

artists.name: D. Tiffany
artists.description:
The enigma of D. Tiffany spans genres, labels, collaborations and communities. Impossible to define yet undeniably recognizable, she has cast her wicked web over the globe; a prolific producer, deeply daring deejay, mover, shaker and tastemaker of the underground; connecting and Canada’s sapphic shapeshifter has been causing waves for over a decade, currently residing in Berlin, she spearheads intriguing and refreshing sounds through her endeavours, Delicate Records, Plush Records Inc, Planet Euphorique & more. Her deep, elevated essence drips into a number of iconic team-ups, which have influenced music connoisseurs everywhere; Ambien Baby, Tapestry of Sound, So Klarity, Mortar & Pestle, Psychedelic Budz, Pillow Queen & Plush Managements Inc., a catalogue of wildly impressive records redefining anything from tripped-out tech and energetic bass laid breaks to grunge and post-punk.

What’s coming next is unpredictable with D. Tiff, but you know for certain it’s always something you need to tune into, a label showcase, a live performance, a secret dj zozi appearance or a new unexpected pair-up that could change the course of dance destiny.

artists.name: Digital Motion Showcase
artists.description:
Digital Motion is led by Blake Motions, a Vancouver-based DJ, and creator of Digital Motion Events, which promotes bass and electronic music culture through curated shows, tours, and community-driven initiatives. Since 2010, the brand has helped shape the city’s underground scene by championing sound system culture and regularly bringing together international and local talent. 

This showcase features Vancouver based artists Adame Twins, Kermode, Konka, & Blake Motions, each drawing from Dubstep, UKG, Drum & Bass, and other subgenres of bass music. From high-energy sets and raw basslines to genre-blending selections and distinct sound design, each artist brings their own approach while contributing to a shared foundation in bass music.

artists.name: D.Mo
artists.description:
Brent D. Mosher, aka D.Mo, is a Toronto-based artist who blends UK Garage & House with his Pacific Northwest bass music upbringing, combining two distinct hubs of the music world into his own unique vision. Through his monthly show - Bits, Beats, and Breaks - on Subtle Radio and on stages across Canada, he continues to carve space for a sub-heavy, breakbeat-infused sound. He has supported artists like Ivy Lab, Machinedrum, Bullettooth, The Glitch Mob, The Librarian, Sherelle, Cesco, Magugu, Kassian, and more.

His taste fuses lush synths, intricate drums, and substantial basslines, seamlessly moving between heavy grooves and ethereal melodies. His distinct take on UK dance music has brought D.Mo to festivals across the country and positions him as one of Canada’s most exciting underground electronic artists.

artists.name: Mercedes
artists.description: Born in the east, raised on the west coast, Mercedes crafts an eclectic blend of east-coast club music, ballroom and much more. With high-energy edits and bubbly rhythms, Mercedes' sets are bound to make you dance.

artists.name: Mood Change
artists.description: With a deep appreciation for the craft of DJing, digging, and sharing music, Mood Change (real name Taylor) seeks out classic and contemporary sounds across genres with a focus on the deep, lush and emotive. A longtime contributor to the Canadian West Coast scene, Taylor has been support for acts such as Lone, Leon Vynehall and Mall Grab, and has performed at festivals like Bass Coast, Shambhala, Rifflandia, and Sled Island. Taylor is also the host of Super Best, a monthly radio show on Subtle Radio that showcases distinctive and lesser-known dance music.

artists.name: Mirage
artists.description: Mirage bends bass frequencies into hypnotic illusions, weaving 140, UKG & Breaks into high‑energy sets built for Bass Coast’s immersive world. A Victoria‑based community builder and founder of Dubversified Culture, he’s spent years shaping West Coast bass culture and amplifying emerging talent. With a monthly show on Subtle Radio (UK), Mirage channels deep, forward‑thinking sound design into performances that captivate dance floors.

artists.name: JPS
artists.description: Jerry Poon (JPS) is a Melbourne-based DJ, curator, and cultural organizer known for shaping the city’s underground music landscape. As a driving force behind The Operatives, JPS has spent over two decades championing forward-thinking club music through influential events, tours, and community initiatives. His DJ sets move fluidly across bass, breaks, hip-hop, and global rhythms, reflecting a deep knowledge of sound system culture while maintaining a sharp, contemporary edge. Both behind the decks and behind the scenes, JPS continues to cultivate spaces that celebrate creativity, diversity, and connection within Melbourne’s music community.

artists.name: Donna Dada
artists.description: Donna Dada is a Vancouver-based DJ whose sets move through UKG, speed garage, jungle,breaks, and bass with ease. Her Trinidadian-Nigerian heritage and long love affair with UK dance culture shape her sound, which is built for the dancefloor. She’s played Bass Coast, Shambhala and Harvest Music Festivals, and has shared the stage with artists including Bakey, Bushbaby, Main Phase, Nia Archives, and Skream + Benga. Whether she's in a club or on an open-air stage, Donna Dada brings a distinct bass-driven energy to the dancefloor.

artists.name: Mike McSuede
artists.description: A fixture in Vancouver’s underground since 1991, Mike McSuede crafts deep, hypnotic house sets, blending minimal and tech sounds with smooth, immersive transitions. A lifelong vinyl collector and curator, Mike’s performances weave timeless records with forward-thinking beats. Beyond the decks, he spent a decade managing Open Studios, supporting Vancouver’s arts scene. With more than 30 years behind the decks, Mike McSuede delivers sets that are deep, musical, and built for the dancefloor.

artists.name: Joia & Sol Elicio
artists.description:
JOIA, the project of Max Huseby, is an evolution in melodic, euphoric soundscapes, moving through hypnotic bass-heavy beats, wild textures, intricate drum rhythms, and uplifting melodies, drawing from breakbeat, two-step, garage, techno, trance, and dubstep.

Sol Elicio is a DJ, producer, and drummer bringing a blend of live energy and electronic beats, moving through breaks, DnB, garage, juke/footwork, house, and techno. With a history of playing Chicago and Detroit house music on vinyl, his sets fuse genres to form new relationships between pieces of music.

artists.name: MARAH
artists.description: Known for her genre-bending productions of Dubstep, trap and Techno, MARAH delivers high-energy, boundary-pushing performances that consistently captivate audiences across North America. MARAH’s music and DJ sets continue to attract both fan and industry attention with her ability to blend genres and heavyweight productions, positioning her as one of the most exciting rising artists in electronic music.

artists.name: Jon Delerious
artists.description:
Hailing from the heartbeat of Calgary, Jon Delerious has been a staple in the West Coast House music scene since the mid 90's.

Jon's music is a vibrant fusion of Deep House, smoked-out Dubby rhythms, and Funky grooves. Some highlight releases include 5 EPs on Nordic Trax and the critically-acclaimed artist album 'No Warning' [2004]. Jon also has releases on Large Music, Worship, Lost My Dog, UM Records, Soulstar, Substation Recordings and more. One thing is certain about Jon, he has carved out a place in D.J. booth and in the studio that is completely his own - he continues to refine his skills as one of Canada's leading electronic musical innovators.

artists.name: INNEZZ
artists.description:
INNEZZ is a Vancouver-based DJ, artist, and curator deeply connected to the Pacific Northwest’s evolving dance music ecosystem. Over more than a decade behind the decks, she has developed a sonic identity shaped by years spent in the local underground as well as on global dance floors and beyond. Her sets are intentionally paced—immersive rather than explosive—and rooted in an understanding of how dancers respond to subtle shifts in energy.

Her entry into the scene began with radio. Early shows across local platforms eventually led to the creation of Azul Radio, a monthly home for exploring minimal, deep textures, and diasporic influences within electronic music. As the show grew, so did its purpose. Azul is now becoming a forthcoming record label, extending its mission to uplift and document the wide range of talent within Vancouver and the Pacific Northwest—a region whose dance music scene is rich, diverse, and deserving of broader recognition.

For INNEZZ, music has always been intertwined with community-building. She has performed across North America at clubs, festivals, and independent spaces, gaining a reputation for sets that reward close listening while still anchoring the floor in steady, compelling motion. Her sound avoids the obvious, instead leaning into nuance—long blends, patient builds, and grooves that reveal themselves over time.

Beyond DJing, her background in visual design and event curation informs how she approaches atmosphere and storytelling. She sees dance floors as collaborative spaces where sound, people, and environment converge—and where intention matters as much as selection.

artists.name: MollyFi
artists.description: Starting on vinyl in the early 2000s, MollyFi built her reputation one record at a time. Known for versatile, crowd-reading sets that move through breakbeats, funk, disco, and 90's dance, she’s spent more than two decades shaping dance floors across Western Canada. A founding member of Girls On Decks (est. 2003), Molly has also helped support generations of DJs while building inclusive spaces through queer events, Pride programming, and community parties.

artists.name: Klippee
artists.description:
Named by Mixmag as an artist bringing new energy to the North American Drum & Bass scene, Seattle-based Klippee is certainly one to watch. His versatile, yet forward-thinking production process yields tracks that truly stand out from the norm. His extensive release discography with powerhouse labels such as Critical Music, Shogun, Lifestyle Music, and more is a testament to his uncompromising will to create.

This exceptionally talented musician has garnered international support from critically-acclaimed producers and pioneers such as Noisia, Kasra, Skeptical, Serum, and many more within the contemporary realms of drum & bass music. His diverse production style has also gained the support of dubstep and bass artists such as Truth, Khiva, and Sicaria Sound. With a vast resume of international performances, his dynamic future-focused drive carries him further and further along his path. Sharing stages with acts like Calyx & Teebee, The Prototypes, and Crissy Criss has proven his ability to captivate a crowd.

The disruptively successful launch of the Seattle-based innovator has caught the eye of both veteran and new drum & bass fans around the world. The next chapter of his career will be the most exciting to date.

artists.name: Lopølo
artists.description:
Lopølo (aka Cory Vanderhoek) is honing his take on techno and house music out of Edmonton, Canada. With precision drum grooves of rumbling propulsion, his tracks crescendo into hypnotic sound design and heady atmospheres showcased on labels like PPRZ Recordings, Heist Mode, and Substation Records, as well as self-released works.

Working with the Umbral Sound System crew and putting on events around the city, Lopølo has been a concrete part of Edmonton’s music scene for well over a decade. His dedication to contributing to the city's scene is paramount to his character. The passion for music and sharing it with people is what drives his output, and the belief that sound can unite people uniquely.

artists.name: Kraang
artists.description:
Originally from Worcestershire, UK (now based in Vancouver), Kraang fuses percussion-driven UK Garage with sub-focused breakbeat energy. His sound draws from a heavy influence of Dubstep, Jungle, and DnB. Expect vibey, air-punching cadences.

Kraang is the co-founder of Odd One Records (with Danny Mal), a Vancouver label that supports local producers, fosters community, and empowers emerging artists. He's deeply involved with BC crews, including Tempo, Insideout, Swrv, and Step Out. His most notable support duties include Oppidan, Bakey and Main Phase.

artists.name: Max Ulis
artists.description: Max has been instrumental in the building of Vancouver’s  underground; his SLAB parties in Vancouver feature the  best in underground House, Techno and contemporary Bass Music. In 2024, Max launched a new party series to feature House music alongside DJ dood. Max has curated for New Forms Festival, Bass Coast Festival, and Public Disco, and he has served as a mentor for many up-and-coming artists in Vancouver. Max’s diverse music productions have found a home on labels such as Turbo, Iile, Club Designs, VRNT Limited, Sloth Boogie and Thirty Year Recordings, and has seen support from notable performers such as Fred P, Ben Sims, Fumiya Tanaka, Prosumer, Laurent Garnier, Roman Flugel, Anz, Soul Clap, and many more.

artists.name: Lon
artists.description:
The Oliver DeShop (All over the shop)/; Spanish: [Lonito] locally: [Lon] (Oliverius Deshopus) House, Tech, Eclectatic dancefloor driven sounds. A domesticated Pemertonous-deejayous-wildous, widely used as a meat and pack animal by party cultures since pre-digital times.

When using a pack, Olivers DeShops can carry about 25% to 30% of their body weight in vinyl records for 8–13km (5–8 miles).

Olivers can live for a period of about 30-60 years, depending on how hard they party. Olivers are very social animals and live with other party people as a herd. The beats produced by an Oliver are percussive, danceable, soft and lanolin-free. Olivers are intelligent and can learn simple tasks after a few repetitions.

artists.name: Limnal
artists.description:
Weaving between notes of ambient dub, experimental techno and broken beat electronica, Limnal is a wide-ranging exploration of deep rhythms, atmospheric soundscapes and leftfield psychedelia. A window into another world, a hazy dreamstate, a story in constant motion; momentary expressions of the ineffable mysteries of being.

Tom can be found co-conspiring with Inside Voices — a collaborative ambient event series based in Nelson, BC — as well as behind the scenes at local rave hubs The Rabbit Hole, Island Wubs, and newly founded underground project SYNDKT, bringing technoid rhythms, mutant jungle and infrasonic bass to the soundsystem under the alias “tsc_”.

artists.name: MetaFloor
artists.description: Metafloor is a key figure in Western Canada’s underground bass music scene, known for genre-fluid blends of bass-driven club music. He has released music on labels including Artikal Music UK, Internal Sounds, Defrostatica, Aufect Platinum, and Deep Dark & Dangerous, and collaborated with artists such as Fixate, Mat The Alien, Doctor Jeep, Coltcuts and Hypho. In 2011, he co-founded Calgary bass collective Sub Chakra, and in 2022, launched Pull Up, now known as Support System - a highly curated, hypnotic rave experience. Whether in the studio or behind the decks, Metafloor explores sound with curiosity, intensity, and a refusal to stay in one lane.

artists.name: Jessu & Pyka
artists.description:
Jessu and Pyka emerged from the digital underground of Twitch, building a dance floor that stretched across time zones and living rooms around the world. What began as late-night streams quickly grew into one of the platform’s most devoted music communities, drawn in by marathon sets, playful energy, and their musical conversations.

Their genre-blending sound, combined with years of experience behind the decks, has allowed them to share elaborate stories on many dancefloors. Their sound moves freely through eras and influences . The warmth of your dad’s record collection, disco and classic house rhythms, and the driving pulse of modern tech house.

As the community grew, the dance floor naturally expanded beyond the screen. The energy, spontaneity, and connection that defined their streams now carry onto club and festival stages, where Jessu and Pyka bring the same feel-good spirit into packed rooms and real dance floors.

artists.name: Mark Woodyard
artists.description: Mark Woodyard’s knowledge of music spans effortlessly across eras and genres. A multi-talented musician who uses looping machines, vocals, guitar and trumpet, he is best known for his wide array of genre-bending covers that showcase his vocal range and eclectic sound, creating truly unique and dance-inspiring performances. With no two shows sounding the same, Woodyard’s improv-esque style captivates audiences and sparks dance floors seconds after taking to the stage.

artists.name: Kozue
artists.description: Kozue is a Canadian-based DJ born and raised in Japan, known for crafting uplifting dance floor journeys through house, deep house, techno, and disco. Blending deep, soulful, organic grooves with electronic textures and psychedelic sounds, her eclectic selections create warm, positive atmospheres that invite dancers into expansive musical storytelling.

artists.name: J to the U
