artists.description: Reggae on wax, J to the U is back again with some Jamaican 45's and other waxy treats for the Big Sound System - Celebrating the sounds that shaped sound system culture! Reggae Church at Bass Coast, Reggae by the river!

artists.name: JPOD
artists.description:
Ever since his first heartbeat, JPOD has had music in his soul. His natural sense of rhythm and movement led to a genuine love for dancing. Equipped with creative motivation and armed with technical knowledge, he continues to articulate an original sound rich with flavour. To keep him dancing, JPOD consistently makes music with punchy beats and bouncy basslines. 

His strong commitment to music with soul, incorporating vintage funk, world, reggae, swing, blues, folk, bluegrass, Latin, Motown, and more! With such a range of styles, JPOD always takes you on a diverse journey that transforms the dance floor. What could be better than smiling and dancing?

artists.name: LeonV
artists.description: Leon V is a Mexican-born DJ, curator, and community organizer based in Vancouver. With nearly two decades behind the decks, he has built a reputation for refined selection and an instinctive understanding of the dancefloor, shaping immersive journeys through minimal, deep house, and groove-driven electronics. As founder of Soma Music Hub and co-founder of MLNDRX, Leon plays an active role in cultivating underground culture and strengthening connections between the Canadian and Mexican electronic music scenes.

artists.name: Slynk
artists.description:
Starting his career in an underground New York-inspired café in his original hometown of Brisbane, Australia, to his first International appearance at Shambhala's Fractal Forest in 2009, he continues to refine his skills, experimenting and educating himself and his fans with mash-up productions of classic hip hop, ingrained with rare and funky, rhythmically savvy grooves.

With the overwhelming support and love from the world’s most respected funky breaks producers behind him, Slynk's reputation has been growing strong with numerous self-releases and with established labels Westwood Recordings, Jalapeno Records, Goodgroove, and Discocakes, just to name a few.

His timeless nu-funk, electro-boogie bravado has met with overwhelming enthusiasm. His Fractal Forest sets brought his unstoppable Forward Momentum to a new level that not only constitutes his best credentials today but also consecrated his name to the West Coast Funk Nation.

Nothing more than another step in spreading his instant, smile-provoking, supreme '80s-funk-flavored boogie, Slynk is now based in Vancouver, British Columbia, to complete his conquering of the New World as an Ambassador of Funk.

artists.name: Sivz
artists.description:
Sivz carves out a distinctive lane in house music, merging the mainstream's emotional pull with the underground's rhythmic grit. Her music lives at the intersection of powerful vocals, percussive edge, and immersive storytelling. Minimalistic yet emotionally rich, it strips house back to its essentials—groove, rhythm, and feeling.

With deep respect for the craft, Sivz is known as a master of set programming, leaving her audiences in drastically different places than where they started. Whether commanding festival stages or intimate club floors, her presence is both captivating and sincere, forging an immediate bond with every listener.

Her productions have earned the ears of established names like Chris Lake and Claptone, with releases on respected labels such as Club Sweat, MoodChild, Rawsome, and Desert Hearts. As her tracks continue to circulate through tastemakers' sets and devoted fans' playlists, Sivz continues on an upward trajectory, maintaining a consistent touring calendar at tastemaker clubs and culture-shaping festivals, with standout performances at marquee gatherings such as Bass Coast, Shambhala, and Friendship.

With precision, emotion, and versatility, Sivz is redefining modern house music, captivating audiences, and raising the bar with every performance.

artists.name: Mt. Doyle
artists.description:
Mt. Doyle is one of Vancouver Island’s most versatile DJs, known for genre-blending sets that move seamlessly between Drum & Bass, House, Funk, Hip-Hop and Bass music, all delivered with a soulful West Coast touch.

Originally emerging from the Drum & Bass scene, pizza in hand, Doyle quickly built a reputation across British Columbia for his musical range and carefully curated mixes. Over the past decade, he has performed at clubs, lounges and major festivals throughout the Pacific Northwest, including Shambhala, Lightning In A Bottle, Rifflandia, Electric Love, Wicked Woods, Astral Harvest, Bamboo Bass, Friendzy Fest and Tall Tree Festival.

Along the way, he has shared the stage with a wide range of international artists, including Aphrodite, Camo & Krooked, Ed Solo, A.Skillz, Stickybuds, The Funk Hunters, Chali 2na, JFB, Dub FX, Rusko, Technimatic, LSB & DRS, Serial Killaz, Dub Pistols, Ray Keith, Netsky and Andy C.

In 2016, Doyle launched The Hydration Mix Series, a monthly Liquid Drum & Bass podcast alongside Akela, Willisist, Erski, Reflektor, Lasu and DJ Surgeon. That same year, he expanded into live performance as a vocalist with Victoria hip-hop band Illvis Freshly, bringing a new dimension to his musical output.

Whether delivering afternoon delights, deep sunrise grooves, or genre-bending dance parties, Mt. Doyle continues to keep crowds moving while pushing beyond stylistic boundaries.

artists.name: panhandle slim
artists.description:
A Haiku by panhandle slim :
record collector
no need to explain much else
pull UP for hot grooves

artists.name: Mat The Alien
artists.description: Mat has DJ’d since 1988. Son of a record store owner. Drummer. Producer. Runs Really Good Recordings, now on its 50th release. Fan of many genres of electronic music. Good friends with The Librarian. Had a bit of a crash on his bike and now uses a wheelchair. Favourite food: butter chicken.

artists.name: Slumbur
artists.description: Pulling from hazy downtempo nostalgia, sauced up r&b, and jazzy flavours of garage, house, dubstep and trap music, settle into something comfy and get tucked into the sounds of Slumbur.

artists.name: NAI
artists.description: NAI is a Vancouver-based DJ whose sound is shaped by her global musical influences, her Syrian heritage, and a connection to dance that runs ancestrally. Her sets are driven by deep percussion, warm bass lines, and soulful melodic threads that weave into something both hypnotic and cinematic. Every performance feels like a journey, transporting listeners to another part of the world and inviting dancers into a shared rhythmic field. Her music is a form of storytelling, a love language of movement and memory, and no two sets ever feel the same.

artists.name: Natty
artists.description: A staple of Victoria's deep house scene, expect to hear many aspects and offshoots of the genre, with room for whatever departures are inspiring him of late. Natty applies a no-rules approach to what works on the dance floor, trading safety for the freedom for us all to align with the energy of the moment. As a commercial real estate broker, he has helped secure permanent homes for several arts and culture venues, a role that has earned him the tongue-in-cheek title of The Night Mayor. He holds a residency at Victoria’s Downbeat club with the event Public Hearing and hosts a regular live broadcast on Sooke Community Radio.

artists.name: SHAASTA
artists.description:
SHAASTA is an emerging DJ and producer based in Whistler, BC, carving out his own lane in the underground electronic scene. Rooted in UK sound system culture, he blends dark dubstep, minimal rolling drum & bass, UKG, 140, and breaks into sets that are heavy, hypnotic, and built for low‑end lovers. His style is defined by bass‑driven energy, clean transitions, and a deep respect for the genres that shaped him.

As the owner of Lost Signal Sound System, SHAASTA brings a hands‑on approach to the culture—building, engineering, and running his own system to support local artists and underground events. His performances reflect both technical skill and a genuine passion for pushing sound system music forward in his community.

Focused, humble, and steadily evolving, SHAASTA is building a foundation rooted in authenticity and craft. Whether behind the decks or behind the system, he delivers a sound that’s raw, immersive, and unmistakably his own.

artists.name: N SO
artists.description:
N SO is a first-generation Uzbek immigrant and has been a driving force in Seattle’s underground since 2015, blending bass, breaks, techno, and club tracks into immersive high-energy sets. Her fluid and experimental style has connected her with a myriad of local collectives, and she is now a member of the DATA-CLUB crew.

She has shared stages with Machinedrum, Teklife crew, Suzi Analogue, Uniiqu3, DJ Godfather and more, driving dancefloors with raw magnetic energy and a penchant for unorthodox blends. As a resident at Kremwerk, she curates Plus One, a monthly b2b party exploring new sounds and inviting selectors to push their boundaries.

Her alias comes from the Zen concept ensō — a circle painted in one uninhibited motion, symbolizing a moment when the mind is free to let the body create. More than just an alias, N SO embodies a feeling, a pulse felt in the moment, leaving you with the resonance of shared rhythm and movement.

artists.name: Smalltown DJs
artists.description:
The Smalltown sound combines the spirit of Canada’s outdoor electronic festivals like Shambhala and Bass Coast with the sound they cultivated at Calgary’s legendary HiFi Club (which they owned and operated).

Mike Grimes and Pete Emes have earned their place in the Canadian and global music scenes. Known for high-energy, genre-bending sets, they’ve been captivating audiences since the early 2000s.

The hallmark of Smalltown DJs is their versatility. Blending house, hip hop, funk, and bass, they create a sound that often defies categorization.

As producers and collaborators, they’ve released tracks on prominent labels such as Fools Gold and Main Course, earning millions of streams. Tracks like Bad Behaviour and Energy highlight their knack for crafting dancefloor anthems. In 2024, they received a Juno nomination for Best Underground Dance Single for Concorde Groove.

As founders of the HiFi Club, they built a creative hub that elevated Calgary’s scene and gave emerging artists a platform.

Smalltown DJs are known for their work ethic, humility, and genuine love for music, which shines through in every set.

With a career spanning over two decades, they continue to push boundaries, inspire others, and represent the best of what the music industry has to offer.

artists.name: Pachinko Machine
artists.description:
Born and raised in Vancouver, Canada, Pachinko Machine is a 25-year-old producer and DJ deeply embedded within the city’s nightlife. Their sound is driven by fast, aggressive drum breaks, nostalgic rave stabs, and East Coast dance culture.

They have performed across Canada and internationally, sharing lineups with artists such as Dazegxd, DJ Swisha, DJ Assault, and Khadija Al Hanafi. A dedicated Junglist, Pachinko Machine aims to platform Jungle wherever they play.

artists.name: RUMPUS
artists.description:
Award-winning DJ and producer RUMPUS is pushing the boundaries of house music, seamlessly blending his productions with live drumming to create a unique and electrifying experience for the dancefloor.

With releases on Insomniac, Club Sweat and Box of Cats, over 10 million streams and multiple Beatport-charting tracks, his music has been featured on Spotify’s top dance music playlists and championed by industry heavyweights like Tiësto, John Summit, Dom Dolla, and Anyma. His track 'Set It Off' was featured in Season 3 of HBO’s hit series 'The White Lotus’, and his viral remix of RUFUS DU SOL's Innerbloom has been played out repeatedly on EDM’s biggest stages, including Tomorrowland, EDC, Coachella and Ultra Music Festival.

Get ready for an exploration into multiple genres of house with the added energy of live drums.

artists.name: PØPU
artists.description:
Javi was born in Madrid (Spain), where from a young age he began his interest in electronic music, taking advantage of the wide scene that the Spanish capital offered him.

When he moved to his current city of residence, Vancouver (Canada), he began to focus professionally as a DJ.

He is currently one of the resident DJs of Vantek Group and Co-founder of one of the most iconic electronic music events that we currently find in the city, FOG.

In addition to playing individually, he is also part of a Spanish electronic music duo called JP-91. 

His sets are characterized by mental atmospheres, groove sounds with hard and hypnotic kick drums transmitting unstoppable energy on the dance floor.

He has also taken his sound to international stages, performing in cities such as Lima (Peru), Havana (Cuba), Tokyo (Japan), Seoul (South Korea), and Bangkok (Thailand), further solidifying his presence within the global underground electronic circuit sharing the stage with artists such as Adriana Lopez, DVS1, Rene Wise, D.Dan, Arthur Robert, Truncate, AADJA, Raven, Julia Govor, Mark Broom, Spartaque, Greggow, Drunken Kong, Jean Terechokva, Behrad Terhani, John 00 Fleming, Stefan Goldman, Drumcell, etc...

artists.name: Shuka
artists.description: Shuka is a DJ & Producer currently based between Edmonton & Vancouver. He has a deep history in the British Columbia & Alberta music scenes, making a notable impression in the Dubstep community over the past decade. Shuka has performed at Shambhala Music Festival, on Rinse FM, Balamii & continues to push his presence in the scene with his own curations and sound system-focused events.

artists.name: ROSS DUPREE [LIVE]
artists.description:
Now based in Vancouver, B.C, Ross cut his teeth DJing in underground clubs in the UK, learning his skills as a producer whilst bouncing between musical cities in Europe. Spending a year in Ibiza, Ross found his sound in the Balearic house and techno whilst mixing his influences from the UK underground and electronica from around the world.

Ross Dupree's live set fuses analog soundscapes, timeless synth nostalgia and machine-driven drum lines. Emotional centre points are woven between deep and dubby electronic sounds that span techno, breaks and house, all with a focus on keeping the dance floor moving.

artists.name: OAKK
artists.description:
OAKK is the brainchild of Vancouver-based singer and producer Cole Edwards. Self-described as “sad boy R&B meets electronica,” OAKK conveys emotion through melodic soundscapes that offer escapism from the mundane — sometimes depressive, sometimes uplifting, always pretty.

In 2024, OAKK released his debut album I Made This For You on Ivy Lab’s imprint TW/TW London. Unbound by genre or expectations, the album revels in creative immediacy, powered by expressive lyrics and progressive composition. With steady releases, a collaboration with Ivy Lab, and a growing catalogue spanning melancholic textures, bedroom-pop, future-soul, chillwave, and DIY influences, OAKK continues to push the boundaries of emotionally driven electronic music.

He has performed at LIB, Shambhala, Okeechobee, Bass Coast, and on a live Boiler Room broadcast, and supported Ivy Lab on their Infinite Falling Ground US tours (Pt. I & II).

artists.name: Sergio SP
artists.description: Toronto-based DJ and producer Sergio SP crafts hypnotic, low-slung dancefloor journeys shaped by nearly two decades in Canada’s underground. His sets weave resonant low-end, spacious atmosphere, and subtle psychedelia, guiding dancers through deep grooves and liminal textures. Moving in the frequencies between places, Sergio builds immersive sonic portals where rhythm, intuition, and collective energy converge.

artists.name: Shiny Things
artists.description:
With 17 years in the profession of sonic sorcery, Vancouver-based DJ & producer Shiny Things has gone from child prodigy of the rave to mystical maverick beaming up entranced audiences to euphoric dimensions. Transforming dancefloors internationally into expansive, technicolored teleportals, Shiny Things seamlessly sculpts slick, innovative soundscapes – real echolocation magick.

With unrivalled drip and a polished penchant for auditory exploration, this Glizzy Wizard conducts every set as an unforgettable odyssey where time slows, and basslines reign. Step into the shimmering realm of Shiny Things for far-out finesse, kaleidoscopic cuts, and exemplary eccentricities.

The Future is Shiny.

artists.name: Pacific Coliseum
artists.description: Pacific Coliseum (aka Teen Daze) is a producer and DJ from Chilliwack, BC, Canada. Since his first release in 2017, Ocean City (Coastal Haze), he's been consistently releasing dreamy, breezy, Balearic House music on various labels; if you've found yourself dancing at an open-air party in the last few years, there's a good chance you've heard one of his tunes. Last summer saw the release of Dreaming In Paradise on London's iconic Rhythm Section International label, with the lead single “Yeah!” landing him a JUNO nomination for Underground Dance Single of the Year. The future is bright too: this year, he'll be releasing more music, including an EP on Permanent Vacation.

artists.name: SATURNA
artists.description: For a wandering mind.

artists.name: Rammie
artists.description: Born in New Jersey to African-American and Indo-Guyanese parents, Rammie gathers the past, present, and future at the center of the dance floor. His sets trace how the Caribbean and Black America built the electronic world, from the roots of Reggae, Dub, and Dancehall to the evolution of UKG, Jungle, Gqom, and Amapiano. By incorporating the Indian soul of his heritage, Rammie draws from one of the world’s first civilizations to bridge the diaspora. Be prepared for a lineage-driven set that hits the chest and makes you move.

artists.name: Passage
artists.description:
Passage (better known in the community as Whitney Caine) is an emerging DJ and producer from Edmonton, Canada. Making connections on the decks as much as she does on the dance floor, her sound creates balance with paradox -- sustained by a foundation of deep bass and rolling breaks.

Whitney is an ever-growing representative of the Umbral Sound System crew, including the host and curator of Umbral’s monthly mix series on Sub FM. As diverse as she is cohesive, Passage juxtaposes grimy and ethereal elements, her underground tastes infused with a pop sensibility all her own.

artists.name: sio
artists.description:
Based in Portland, sio approaches DJing as a form of storytelling. Her sound draws from a wide range of influences, from R&B and global rhythms to a deep love for jazz, hip hop, and expressive instrumentation. She brings a strong melodic thread into everything she plays, exploring different genres with a natural sense of progression. 

She describes her selections as pages from her journal, small reflections of what she’s experiencing and the love that she holds. That personal connection comes through in the way her sets are arranged, the pacing, and the moments that invite you to stay a little longer.

artists.name: Tsimka & Michael Red
artists.description:
Tsimka & Michael Red is a collaboration of music from the land and from the heart (and knowing and listening).

Tsimka sings, speaks and plays the drum (and other percussion). Most lyrics are in Tsimka's heritage language Tla-o-qui-aht ƛaʔuukʷiʔatḥ, drawing from cultural teachings and love for her home, and expressions of the human experience like joy, desire, resilience, care and kinship.

Michael fills out the rest of the music, beats and atmospheres *all* created from blending & morphing & effecting natural sounds recorded primarily on Tsimka's ƛaʔuukʷiʔatḥ home territory. Sounds like Cixwatsac (Frank Island frogs), Swainson's Thrush, and other birds. A little creek at Hiłwinʔis, ocean, rain, mist and ice. Tsimka's voice and drum, and other materials she might play (shells, salal, a rattle, etc.), are also heavily affected and manipulated. Recordings of live improvised dub & soundscape sessions are continuously recycled back into their productions as well, as their sound continues to evolve.

Deep meditative soundscapes and atmospheres, song and energy .. inspired by dub, nature and non-physical life .. with a featured element of ƛaʔuukʷiʔatḥ language expression & restoration.

Tsimka: vocals, drums, sound objects (shells, salal, rattle,...), live vocal processing
Michael Red: live dubbing of pre-recorded stems, live & spontaneous vocal processing and looping, other layers of sound & live manipulation

The primary source sounds of *all* audio heard is from either Tsimka's voice, Tsimka's drum, or field recordings Tsimka made in her home Nuučaan̓uł Territory and some made in Sḵwx̱wú7mesh Territory.

artists.name: The Silk Road
artists.description: Explore the world with Yelda and Biplov – an Afghan and Nepalese DJ duo inspired by the true-life historic Silk Road that wound its way through Europe, Asia, and Africa. They combine forces to create a journey that showcases instruments and languages from across the world in an effort to spark cultural curiosity and inclusivity. Their musical style favours a variety of South Asian & Middle Eastern vocals combined with rhythmic dance beats & eclectic basslines. Their fluid approach mixes global sounds with Hip Hop, Rap, Amapiano, Afrobeat, House, Jersey Club, Dancehall, Reggaeton, Deep Dub, and other bass-heavy genres.

artists.name: tiny keekz
artists.description: Kiana, aka tiny keekz, has built a foundation balancing intricate drums, booty-shaking bass, and powerful female energy in every performance. She specializes in high-energy club music, UKG and drum & bass.

artists.name: Tony H
artists.description: Tony H is a Panama-born DJ/Producer, now based in Seattle, WA. In 2015, he founded his own label, Late Night Munchies, then in 2019 started up Munchies After Dark, a sub-label primarily focusing on melodic house and techno. Tony's energetic attitude and live performance skills have always been a powerhouse in the scene, as they are shining and breaking through to the forefront today. Kevin Knapp is on record saying Tony was an “Artist to Watch” in 2021, and that held true into 2022 when he was put on the “10 Rising Black Artists You Should Know” in Gray Area Magazine, and he was most recently featured by Splice as a producer to watch in 2026. Aside from founding his own labels, Tony has releases on Desert Hearts, Dirtybird, Space Yacht, Music Is 4 Lovers, Flashmob Records, DND Recs, Farris Wheel, and so many more. He has made a name for himself as one of the most thrilling up-and-coming artists in the house music scene. and has had buzzworthy performances at Coachella, Miami Music Week, Day Trip Seattle, Beyond Wonderland, Bass Coast Festival and many others.

artists.name: ZDBT b2b Syd Woz
artists.description:
ZDBT is the moniker of producer, DJ, and live performer Zachary David Boynton Treble, working within the underground electronic music scene. Syd Woz is a DJ, event curator, and emerging producer known for fluid, intuitive mixing, moving through early 90s house and techno alongside contemporary cuts.

Their recent collaboration includes a debut release on Short Span Records. Based in Vancouver, the pair also run sessions out of their shared studio, Deep Blue, where much of their mixes and productions take shape.

artists.name: Xtine
artists.description: Xtine, a Canadian DJ from Ottawa, has become a defining presence in Vancouver’s underground music culture. She found raving in her TMU years and has spent the last 11 years in BC’s underground spaces. From throwing events with the former Babelink collective, a show on n10.as, and working as creative lead at District, she’s built a career curating many perspectives in electronic music. Xtine has been found at Bass Coast Festival, Igloofest, Dolly, B-Side Radio, Vantek, LUL and beyond. Find her weaving hypnotic, ethereal minimal tech, acid, breaks, house and techno; second star to the right and straight on till morning.

artists.name: Tailor Jae
artists.description: Tailor Jae is an East London DJ and Producer specialising in bass-heavy sets. Coining her sound as “genreless at heart, heavy on the bass”, she plays what she wants. A solid figure in UK music and renowned for her raw approach to DJing and production, she continues to defy expectations and break rules. Not one to fit in but stand out, she is a Mixmag DJ competition winner and a breakthrough DJ Mag nominee. As well as this, she has had defining sets on Boileroom, residencies on BBC 1Xtra, cosigns from Deep Medi and is the founder of her own imprint Heavy On The Bass. Tailor has had releases on leading bass labels - Manuka, Clasico, UTTU and SZNS7N and continues to shake up the industry with her HOTB Edits series.

artists.name: Bakey
artists.description:
London-born and Leeds-based Freddie Baker started to turn heads back in 2018 with Cookie — a moody rap collaboration with BNC Javen, which he co-produced at just 17 years old. Since then, he’s released music on labels such as Warehouse Project Records, Twenty Twenty London, and Time Is Now. His Bring It Back EP was listed by Mixmag as one of the best EPs of 2021.

Bakey’s passion isn’t confined to the four walls of his studio. He is just as zealous when it comes to DJing and has brought tremors to dance floors around the world, including an iconic Boiler Room set in 2022, as well as performances at Glastonbury, Boomtown, Bass Coast, Outlook, Love Saves the Day, Waterworks, and We Out Here.

The past 12 months have seen Bakey complete tours in Australia, New Zealand, and India (alongside his brother, Breaka), be interviewed and featured on BBC Radio 1, stream for DJ Mag HQ, and perform at Europe’s newest bucket-list club, Openground.

artists.name: Stargirl
artists.description:
Stargirl is a Vancouver-based DJ who draws inspiration from underground and dance club energy, ultimately igniting her passion for unifying music with artistic expression. Combining elements of UKG, Breaks, UK Bass and Speed Garage, each set is an entirely new experience each time she hits the decks.

Within this last year, she’s shared the stage with artists such as Osmosis Jones, In-Parallel and Camoufly, showcasing her unique understanding of how to connect with her audience through bouncy melodies and bass-heavy bangers. Renowned for her dynamic curation and distinctively high-energy sets, she continues to prove her peer-given title as one of the most vibrant selectors in the scene.

artists.name: Social Club
artists.description: Upcoming shellist from Shanghai, now based in Vancouver, 21-year-old Social Club brings only the highest energy to the dance floor. Known for his heavy-hitting, fast-tempo 4x4 tracks, his tunes have gained serious traction in the Garage scene worldwide. Expect non-stop energy, rumbling basslines, percussive pressure and sharp blends while he’s behind the decks.

artists.name: Tor
artists.description:
Tor has built a distinct sound in electronic music through his blend of intricate percussion, emotive melodies, and immersive atmospheres. Drawing from downtempo, world music, and contemporary electronic influences, his work balances refined production with heartfelt expression, creating music that resonates on both emotional and physical levels.

With a focus on sophisticated rhythm and texture, Tor’s sets and productions invite listeners on a journey through evolving tempos and moods, uniting introspection with dancefloor energy. Recognized for his detailed sound design and thoughtful approach to performance, he continues to craft music that connects deeply, both in the studio and on stage.

artists.name: The JROD Annual Arm Wrestling Championship
artists.description:
Why wait for Monday morning to conquer Bass Coast when you can be the champ all weekend, lugging around your trophy to assert dominance over your puny-armed foes? Past champs have gone on to play a main stage slot and hold up an A&W.

Joining the team this year on the mic is former champion and esteemed arm-wrestling aficionado Denise the Beast!

artists.name: Westerley
artists.description:
Westerley is a music maker and DJ based on the West Coast of British Columbia. His sound is heavily influenced by the vibe and aesthetic of Classic Dubstep and Roots Dub. As a producer, his original music has gained recognition and has been played live and on the radio by some of the most respected artists in the dubstep/bass music community. As a DJ, he is backed with dubplates from some of the top players in the scene.

Over the past two and a half decades, he has performed at countless events in North America and abroad and has become well known for late-night sets at festivals and after-hour venues.

artists.name: Sweet Anomaly
artists.description:
Sweet Anomaly {the rattle, the field,}
Montreal/Sunshine Coast, Canada

Sweet Anomaly is a Sunshine Coast-based DJ and producer. Born and raised in Montreal, a culturally diverse and vibrant city, he was exposed to a wide variety of music from a young age. The inspiration he drew from these experiences shapes and influences his sounds to this day. 

Over the last 25 years, he has played alongside many great artists at countless festivals up and down the West Coast and beyond.

artists.name: Willisist
artists.description:
Deep, rolling DnB. Heavy thick 85. Blissful liquid and half time. 
Close your eyes and listen closely, there's layers. 

Since 2007, The Willisist has been pushing the underground in Vancouver. As the head of the SHAHdjs, he's forged the legendary False-o-Ween series, some of the best New Year's parties ever, hosted over 300 shows across many venues, pushed the local scene on B.Side Radio, Nautilus, the Back Stage Lounge and more. He’s been a staple at Bass Coast festival, with sets at Wicked Wood, Shambhala, and many other outdoor BC events.    

With a genuine love for Drum and Bass, he has opened or hosted for the likes of Calibre, D-Bridge, Andy C, Dub FX, Dub Phizix, Blu Mar Ten, LSB, Spectrasoul, Breakage, High Contrast, Pendulum, and dozens more.

Tune in, let's ride.

artists.name: Wake the Town
artists.description: Wake the Town is a vibrant dancefloor experience rooted in Portland, Oregon. Led by DJs Barisone, Danny Corn, and PRSN, Wake the Town moves through a variety of genres and tempos, celebrating the rich tapestry of sounds that define modern bass music. Wake the Town has built it's reputation on featuring cutting-edge artists from across the globe, hosting international events, and earning recognition from publications like Mixmag.

artists.name: Tempo DJs
artists.description:
These lads are all about digging deep into House and Garage. From disco edits to grimy basslines, if it brings a smile to their faces, they'll play it.

This is a full-circle moment for Vancouver-based British/Irish duo Tempo DJs, who first met on the Slay Bay dancefloor at Bass Coast 2022.

Since then, they've run events under the Tempo name in Vancouver, which have featured legends like Bakey and Marc Cotterell, and, as Tempo DJs, they've supported heavy hitters like Conducta, Silva Bumpa, and Introspekt, among others.

artists.name: WERK
artists.description: Vancouver-based music producer and DJ WERK has been unlocking new methods to translate ideas into personally affecting experiences through his music. His wacky, lively & anthemic style is a careful mix of polished sounds developed from years of practicing the craft. Pulling influence from techno, grime, house, rap, and classic rave sounds.

artists.name: Thomas Workshop
artists.description:
Immersed in jazz and folk since toddler times and fuelled by his passion for music, Soren has been DJ-ing under the alias 'Thomas Workshop' while in cohesion touring as a founding member, vocalist, writer and producer of Lazy Syrup Orchestra. 

Additionally, he co-produces events with Soul Hop Productions. Hosting artists in VanCity and co-creating dance opportunities. With LSO, Soren is performing on an array of festival stages as the group continues to evolve and develop new music. He’s happiest when surrounded by aquatic house music, basketball and plant-based hygiene products.

artists.name: Skerd
artists.description: Originally from the Netherlands and now residing in Vancouver, Skerd carefully curates her sets with dubstep selections from both European and North American roots. With her signature multi-genre approach and saucy blends, she infuses an electric vibe onto the dance floor that keeps the crowd locked in all night long.

artists.name: Proper Fry Up
artists.description:
Proper Fry Up serves up high-energy selections packed with bass, bounce, and good-time energy. Known for blending playful edits, wobbly basslines, and festival-ready grooves, his sets are designed to keep dancefloors moving from the first drop to the final track.

Being part of the Blissfields festival team in the UK before moving across the world and becoming immersed in the Australian underground scene, before relocating to the Sea-to-Sky corridor in 2019, Proper Fry Up developed a sound rooted in UK bass culture, house, and underground club music. His sets fuse chunky low-end with cheeky edits with high-impact drops that keep crowds locked in.

Now based in British Columbia, Proper Fry Up has supported artists including Bensley, Kytami & Phonik Ops, Father Funk, Pino & Lobe and Neon Steve, showcasing the versatility of his sets. He is also a member of the team behind Spacedaze Festival, where he contributes as both an MC and performer, helping shape the festival’s high-energy atmosphere on and off the stage.\where he contributes as both an MC and performer, helping shape the festival’s high-energy atmosphere on and off the stage.

artists.name: Ivy Lab
artists.description: Ivy Lab, now the duo of Gove Kidao & J. Fogel, emerged from North London’s bass scene blending hip-hop, leftfield bass, and UK sound system culture. Founders of the acclaimed 20/20 LDN label and club night—hailed as “ultra-innovative” (TimeOut) and a “London standout” (Mixmag)—they’ve earned honors like DJ Mag’s Best Breakthrough Label. Their albums Death Don’t Always Taste Good and Infinite Falling Ground cemented their global reputation, with performances at Coachella, Glastonbury, and Red Rocks. In 2025, Ivy Lab return to their Drum & Bass roots with new club releases and tours across the US and Europe.

artists.name: Iggy
artists.description:
Iggy is a DJ and producer from British Columbia's Sea to Sky region. Known for crafting immersive, driving sets that move between dark minimal, psychedelic and melodic techno. With a refined ear for progression and energy, his performances guide listeners through hypnotic and evolving soundscapes.

A respected figure within the local scene, Iggy has played a key role in shaping community-driven events and serves as a director of Space Daze Festival. His presence across Whistler’s club and event circuit has seen him support and share lineups with internationally recognized artists in the global dance music scene.

Whether in an intimate setting or on a larger stage, Iggy delivers focused, high-energy sets built around tension, groove, and atmosphere - designed to keep the crowd locked in from start to finish.

artists.name: Sarkasm
artists.description:
Joseph Bondoc (SARKASM) is a Filipino DJ built on rhythm and groove. Shaped by deep & soulful house, footwork, ghetto house, Baltimore & Jersey club, budots, and many more, SARKASM weaves in his roots with nostalgic cuts along the way. 

Heavily influenced by his choir background, SARKASM ties in years of training and musicality within his identity as an artist. SARKASM will take you through a soulful, yet high-energy cross-genre experience.

artists.name: Duel of the Poets: An interactive poetic duel on typewriters
artists.description:
Workshop Description
A fast-paced, analogue performance where poetry becomes sport and creation happens in real time.

Two poets. Two typewriters. One crowd.

Facing each other in a live poetic duel, Daniel Zuccarello and North take three spontaneous topics from the audience and race against the clock to write original poems on vintage typewriters. A third orator recites classic poems while they type. Each round ends in a live performance and audience voting. Audience-driven, participatory, and theatrical, this workshop blends humour, improv, and heart.

Facilitator Biographies
Daniel Zuccarello is a performance poet, event producer, and creative facilitator who hosts poetry events at festivals. His work transforms poetry into shared, playful experiences.

Cailan Maurutto is a storyteller, sailor, and poet whose life has been shaped by the sea. After years spent restoring and living aboard a salvaged sailboat, he now travels wherever the wind and inspiration take him, collecting stories from forgotten harbours, chance encounters, and distant shores. Blending wit, heart, and adventure into every performance, Cailan brings an authentic voice that reminds audiences there's poetry hidden in even the roughest waters. When he's not chasing the horizon, he's inviting others to laugh, reflect, and embrace the unexpected.

Since 2010, North has been part of the music scene through volunteering and supporting local artists and events. A longtime music lover with a passion for poetry, they enjoy the way music and words bring people together and create real connection. Over the years, they’ve stayed involved in creative spaces that celebrate expression, community, and culture.

artists.name: Movement Healing
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Expand, elevate & transform movement into healing. 

Jeanette Kotowich shares an experiential dance workshop, inspired by Indigenous cosmology, intercultural exchange, expressive sovereignty, and futurity. 

Connecting with the natural and Spirit world, we will explore the 4 elements (earth, fire, water, air) through embodiment and exploration. We will experience our own humanity through sensing into our animal body and tap into joy with authentic movement, dancing to curated sonic textures that will get your bones moving and your heart opening. 

Led by Nêhiyaw/Métis dance artist, Jeanette Kotowich, originally from Treaty 4 territory, Saskatchewan, who resides as a guest on MST territories. 

All body types, ages and skill levels are welcome. No previous dance experience is necessary. The only requirement is to bring your courageous heart and openness to explore something new. 

Facilitator Biography
Jeanette Kotowich is a dance artist, facilitator, and choreographer. Jeanette creates and self-produces work that reflects Nêhiyaw/Métis cosmology within the context of contemporary dance, Indigenous performance, and Indigenous futurism. A professional artist for 15 years, their works have toured nationally across Turtle Island and internationally to Australia and Germany. After developing their Movement Healing workshop in 2019, they have been facilitating it nationally and internationally to all types of communities. They reside as a guest on the Ancestral and unceded Sḵwx̱wú7mesh, Səl̓ilw̓ətaʔɬ/, and Xʷməθkʷəy̓əm territories, colonially known as Vancouver.

artists.name: Seed Stewardship & Sharing
artists.description:
Workshop Description
This workshop centres on the importance of seeds in sustaining ta tmícwa (the land) and our relationships. Participants will learn plant and seed identification, ethical harvesting practices, and practical techniques for cleaning both wet and dry seeds.

The session concludes with a community seed swap, and participants are encouraged to bring seeds to share and exchange.

Facilitator Biography
Shayla Chalifoux (muskwasis) is a St’át’imc, Nêhiyaw, and mixed European horticulturalist based on Coast Salish Territory. She is the founder of Shaylish Plants, a native plant nursery and consulting business, and works toward Indigenizing plant spaces for Tákem i snek̓wnúk̓wa7lhkalha (All Our Relations).

artists.name: Flow Within
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Flow Within is an invitation to reconnect with the flow of your being & emotions through mindful movement, breathwork, and a deep connection to the Earth. Rooted in the Svādhiṣṭhāna (sacral) centre, the practice emphasises gentle, fluid movement and is guided by breath and your inner rhythm - like a quiet river shaping itself to the land. With spacious pauses for listening and integration, this class supports nervous system regulation, emotional awareness, and a grounded, embodied flow.

Guided by South Asian facilitation- this workshop combines ancient practices with modern restorative techniques, helping you reconnect with your body, breath, and the land.

Through the South Asian lens, you'll explore pranayama breathwork to deepen awareness and cultivate inner peace. Through our connection to the land you’ll honour the Earth as a living being and tune into the rhythms of nature.  Together we will cultivate an integrative healing space rooted in reverence and presence.

Bring a mat, a yoga strap, a pen and journal (fuzzy socks, light blanket, and eye mask optional).

Facilitator Biographies
Harmeet Kaur is a mother and a Yoga Therapist (C-IAYT & E-RYT 500), and wellness advocate blending Ayurveda, Yoga, and breathwork through a trauma-informed, decolonial lens. She is co-founder of the Indigenous Yoga Collective and is committed to accessible wellness, community building, and reconnecting BIPOC individuals to ancestral wisdom. Harmeet views her work as a love letter to life and is passionate about pleasure activism, healing, and justice.

artists.name: Mushroom Medicine Mastery
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Step into the hidden intelligence of the fungal kingdom.

In this immersive class, herbalist Yarrow Willard weaves science, folklore, and lived experience to reveal mushrooms as powerful allies for food, medicine, and human resilience. Together, we will explore the top medicinal mushrooms, how they function in the body, and why they are uniquely suited to support immune health, nervous system balance, vitality, and resilience in the face of many of the most challenging diseases of our time.

You’ll journey beneath the forest floor to understand why mushrooms appear, how they operate as ecological and neurological knowledge networks, and what makes them exceptional agents of healing and regeneration.

Drawing on over 20 years of experience working with medicinal mushrooms commercially, clinically, and in the wild, Yarrow illuminates the often-misunderstood world of fungal intelligence, bridging ancient wisdom with modern science.

This class is an invitation to see mushrooms not just as organisms, but as teachers, protectors, and myco-warriors of vitality, adaptation, and renewal.

Facilitator Biography
Yarrow Willard (Herbal Jedi) is a second-generation Clinical/Master Herbalist, Co-Founder of Harmonic Arts, and Director of the Wild Rose College of Herbal Medicine. Known as the Herbal Jedi on YouTube, he is an educator, storyteller, and herbal ambassador who inspires people to reclaim their health and deepen their connection with the natural world.

With an affinity for functional mushrooms and a lifelong love of plant medicine, Yarrow blends science and spirit, roots and reason, tradition and play. His teachings combine time-honoured healing systems with modern research, creating an innovative and accessible approach that empowers people to care for themselves and the Earth.

artists.name: Neil Jam3s
artists.description: Astrocolor frontman and half of Righteous Rainbows of Togetherness, NEIL JAM3S DJ sets are a sonic journey through the organic sounds of house, dub, lofi, cumbia and afrobeat. Neil pulls from various corners of the world, both crate digging and in his original tracks, to build cosmic tapestries that weave rhythms and sounds, pulling inspiration from new and burgeoning producers and artists from across the globe.

artists.name: ConfiDance
artists.description:
Workshop Description
ConfiDance is a workshop for those who desire to be more confident dancing in social settings. In this workshop, you will learn basic dance moves that you can make your own, you will become more comfortable moving your body in public, and you will build confidence in fully expressing yourself. This is a workshop to empower people to move their bodies more in a way that feels good to them, without judgement or thinking it needs to look a certain way. By the end of this workshop, you will feel more confident dancing, learn dance moves that you can take with you and unlock another level of self-expression.

Facilitator Biography
Pearl Cicci is a somatic health coach, biohacker, and sober raver on a mission to bring health and consciousness into the party and festival scene. She helps ravers and creatives optimise their well-being through simple daily habits that create natural highs, next-level energy, and improved mental states. Using her signature LIT Body Blueprint, Pearl blends mind, body, soul, and heart for a holistic approach to peak-state living through the body. A passionate dancer, she believes movement is one of the most powerful biohacks for wellbeing. Pearl’s philosophy: partying can support our health, so we can truly rave to the grave.

artists.name: Pathways from settler-colonialism to right-relationship
artists.description:
Workshop Description
A welcoming, compassionate, and honest container where we look at the historic processes and present day conditions that create and maintain settler colonialism. We’ll talk about what we can do, individually and collectively, to work toward right relationship. We share big and small direct asks from Indigenous communities, and ask you to consider which ones you can start honouring now and which ones you can work toward. We end with a voluntary ceremony to commit to any actions you want to take going forward.

Facilitator Biographies
Tsimka is a Tla‑o‑qui‑aht citizen working in language revitalization, cultural education, environmental restoration, group hosting, and facilitation. She enjoys working in spaces of openness to broaden understanding among others while continuing her own learning. For many years, she facilitated conversations about culture, colonization, and environment with visitors to her territory.

Jeanette is an educator, researcher, and activist focused on collective liberation since 2010. Rooted in hope and delight, she facilitates small and large groups, courses, and events centred on creating a more loving and just world. She excels at supporting people through difficult conversations while remaining grounded in inherent goodness and the potential for transformation. Her main projects include Pathways to Right Relationship with Tsimka and Spit Fire Press.

artists.name: Rave and Rest: Yang into Yin Yoga
artists.description:
Workshop Description

The Best of Both Worlds. First, stoke your inner fire in a playful, dynamic yang practice set to a decadent selection of hype tunes. Explore your edges and build heat while focusing on strength and cultivating your inner warrior. Then, use that heat to melt into a yin practice, set to a soft, serene ambient soundtrack to take you deep within, bringing in the sweet waters of self-love and compassion.

This session explores emotional regulation, nervous system restoration, and the power of sweet surrender. The practice will close with a long savasana and guided relaxation. All bodies are welcome. Options will be provided for everyone.

Facilitator Biography

Jassy Blue brings a lighthearted, accessible approach to yoga while guiding participants on a deep inner journey that often feels therapeutic. With over 12 years of practice and training, including a foundational teacher training completed in India at Trimurti in 2018, Jassy weaves self-inquiry and empowerment into every class.

Jassy's sessions integrate both yin and yang energies, encouraging students to connect with their inner teacher, strength, and medicine. Each offering is grounded in the belief that yoga is both a homecoming and a tool for personal sovereignty.

Expect a joyful, embodied experience designed to leave you feeling grounded, open, and empowered.

artists.name: Countering the Manosphere with Empathy and Media Literacy
artists.description:
Workshop Description
An interactive presentation that explains the rise of the manosphere through a systematic analysis of disinformation and capitalism. Participants will leave with a deeper understanding of how it operates and practical tools to respond as a community, working toward a brighter and more hopeful future.

Together, we’ll unpack questions such as:
• What is the manosphere and how did it emerge?
• Who is being impacted and how are they being reached?
• How are its core ideas spreading into mainstream culture?
• What is the antidote to the manosphere? 

Facilitator Biography
Aaron is a passionate secondary school educator and community builder with a deep understanding of media literacy, disinformation, and the world today.

artists.name: Immersion: Sound, Mind, Body
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Dawn and her husband Craig have been sharing Immersion with the Bass Coast community for the past 6 years. They are excited to return and take you on a visceral journey through sound, movement and stillness. This unique immersive experience invites you to dive Into the Deep Blue and connect with what lies beneath the surface. 

In this 90-minute experience, Craig mixes music and sound live while Dawn leads a Flow followed by Yin, ending with Yoga Nidra techniques and a deep Savasana while Craig bathes you in sound. This movement is coupled with electronic music, natural sounds, visceral tonal vibrations, delta waves and binaural beats to stimulate your senses and facilitate mind-body integration. 

This duo designed Immersion with four high-fidelity speakers to create an immersive three-dimensional soundscape. You not only hear the sound, you feel it envelop your senses. This is more than yoga with music; they have curated emotive music and sound to take you on a journey inwards, moving towards a deep meditative state. 

Immersion is an interactive, movement and sound experience - a shared energetic experience by all who participate.

Facilitator Biography
Dawn began her teaching career in 2010 in Calgary, Alberta and is a well-known yoga and meditation teacher with over 15 years of full-time teaching. Yoga brought Dawn and Craig together; they met in Calgary 15 years ago at Yoga Santosha, where Dawn was teaching. In October 2021, they moved to Victoria, where Dawn continues to teach. 

Dawn teaches at top yoga studios and offers private and corporate sessions. She is a 500RYT and certified Meditation Teacher and teaches a variety of styles from Flow to Hatha to Yin and Restorative Yoga, as well as Breathwork and Meditation. In addition to Immersion, Dawn has facilitated several Meditation and Pranayama workshops.

Yoga truly changed her life, and teaching is her passion; Dawn is devoted to helping people feel good and feel more at ease in body and mind. She is known for clear and concise cueing, accessible, mindful, grounding and balanced classes integrating breath and movement; she also blends music and meditation to create a serene experience.

Craig has decades of music and event experience, which allowed a perfect opportunity to collaborate on a project that would express their shared passions. Having both experienced the meditative and healing power of music and sound, the idea of immersion emerged.

artists.name: LIGHT BLUES: SOUND SESSION
artists.description:
Workshop Description
A sound journey, very much in the key of deep blue, weaving together natural sounds with special frequencies & instrumentation and emotive soundscapes. Meditative, expansive, deepening and unwinding. Gentle and allowing, while also shifting a bit of energy when we can. Warm waves of bass from whales and deep earth. Rich evolving tones from a healthy heart. High sparkles from birds and etheric light. No experience necessary. Just come and lay down or sit comfortably, and be as you are. You could close your eyes or soft gaze into the trees, and go as deep as you feel. This could be a great context for some focused inner-work, some light stretching, journalling or doodling, or simply allowing yourself to let go and drift. This workshop will be guided, very minimally with some voice, so we can get comfortable and ease in, have some moments and go some places, take our time to come out of the experience, and then have some extra time safe-in-sound once we are done, so we can ease out nicely.

Facilitator Biography
Michael Red is a Vancouver-based DJ/producer/performer well known to the Bass Coast community for dancefloor-centred sets, but also with a fully flowered ambient music & sound wellness side as well. Michael's been working with nature sounds and special frequencies, woven together thru electronics and intuition, for a few decades now - facilitating live sound journeys and creating customized mixes for various therapeutic uses and releasing original works as well. Michael recently initiated 'Light Blues' as an umbrella name for all his sound wellness work and also for his modern ambient work that fully functions in the wellness space.

artists.name: Deep Dive: Flowy Hatha for Groove-ready Hips
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Unlock your second chakra and free up your hips with this well-balanced hatha/flow class. Set to a feel-good, vibe-forward playlist, we’ll rinse out long, deep holds with watery, creative flows. Come free your body so that it can be a conduit to receive and integrate messages, emotion and life force!

Facilitator Biography
Emily is a well-loved and respected yoga instructor who has been teaching since 2009. Raw and authentic, Emily is known for her delicate spiritual insights, intelligent sequencing, and powerful asana classes. She has led over 20 local and international yoga retreats with her dear friend and co-facilitator, Susan Horning. Emily currently supports pregnant and birthing people as a certified midwife. When she isn’t moving her body or catching babies, she can be found snuggling with her partner and her two littles.All bodies, ages, and experience levels are welcome. No prior yoga experience is required. The only invitation is to arrive with openness and a willingness to explore, move, and reconnect.

artists.name: Lino Block Stamping
artists.description:
Workshop Description 
This class will teach people proper stamping techniques using custom Bass Coast lino blocks. We will have a variety of stamps available and help people use brayers and fabric ink to customise their purchased merch or previously owned rave wear. 

Facilitator Biographies
To the Cooney sisters, it’s always been about creation, crafting, and making art. As children, Angela and Andrea would surround themselves in colour and weirdness, always having each other to brainstorm the next fantastical idea. Over the years, the girls grew together as sisters, best friends and designers. With a keen interest in upcycled, discovered, and unconventional materials, Angela and Andrea have created a unique aesthetic that blurs the boundaries of art and fashion without losing the youthful feel of two sisters creating their own world for the simple joy of it.

artists.name: The Power of Observation: Discover Nature with iNaturalist
artists.description:
Workshop Description
What’s that plant? That bug? That mushroom? This interactive workshop introduces participants to iNaturalist, a global citizen science app that helps people learn about their environment while contributing real data to conservation science. The session includes a short indoor tutorial, followed by an outdoor exploration.


No experience necessary, just bring a phone or camera and curiosity. Abby will share how the act of noticing can transform your relationship with the natural world. It’s like Pokémon GO, but for budding naturalists.


This workshop will be held at 1pm on Thursday and Saturday, beginning at the Slowtempo Stage with a presentation ( approx. 45 minutes), then venturing out onto the land to discover and identify the nature around us through iNaturalist ( approx. 45 minutes). This workshop will be approximately 1.5 hours long.


Facilitator Biography
Abby is an iNaturalist ambassador with deep ties to BC’s ecological field. Originally from Indonesia, she moved to Canada in 2016 to study Geography and Environmental Studies at the University of Victoria. She has worked as a native plant consultant and team lead for the BC Biodiversity iNaturalist Team, and is now pursuing a master’s in Industrial Ecology. Her greatest joy is helping others experience the wonder of citizen science.

Julia is a naturalist from Victoria now based in Vancouver. Her love for iNaturalist sprouted while working for BC Parks as a student ranger, and continued to grow during her 3 years on the BC Biodiversity iNaturalist Team. Now working in environmental consulting, Julia spends much of her free time iNatting and observing the nature all around her. There is so much to discover!

artists.name: Hear For Life: Your guide to hearing protection
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Education about hearing protection is common in noise-exposed workplaces, but recreational environments often leave people in the dark. Foam earplugs or canal caps? Custom-moulded or pre-moulded? Class A or B? What does 85 dBA Lex even mean?

Join Hardeep for a clear overview of the risks of noise exposure, the various hearing protection options available, how to choose the right one, and how to use and care for it correctly.

Facilitator Biography
Hardeep is an industrial audiometric technician, physiotherapist, and the owner of a multidisciplinary health clinic in northern British Columbia. With over 15 years of experience, he is committed to helping people live their most functional lives. His passion for education and safety makes this workshop both informative and empowering.

artists.name: Lorne B
artists.description: A purveyor and selector of underground dance music in Western Canada, Lorne B continues to push both community and sound. Known to run the gauntlet of genres for the dancefloor, he plays sets that blur the lines between electronic, house, dub, and techno. Playing alongside dance music heavyweights at home and abroad, Lorne B strives to be an ambassador for Western Canada's underground scene.

artists.name: Drug Noise
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Discover what is and what is not in drugs used at festivals.In a data driven discussion, find out how and why people are choosing to alter their consciousness on the dancefloor. Distilling insights from the internationally renowned drug checking services of Bass Coast and Shambhala, we paint a picture that discerns myths from reality.Answer questions: How strong is my molly? Do I have (R) or (S) ketamine? Does acid go bad? Where do my drugs come from? If I combine this with that, what will happen? And more. This is an opportunity to ask questions you may not want to type into google.

Facilitator Biographies
Antoine Marcheterre leads drug checking for Interior Health and brings more than a decade of expertise to the field. He oversees services at both Bass Coast and Shambhala, guiding data analysis and educational efforts. Antoine is particularly drawn to the rich overlap between history, chemistry, and human behavior—and he’s always keen to explore how these worlds shape the way we understand substances today. The Interior region of BC has long been a crucible of innovation when it comes to implementing drug checking services.

Jarred lives in Victoria, BC, where he enjoys wearing many hats. One hat he wears has a propeller on the top. Another hat has a pestle and mortar off the side. The other hat, weirdly enough, has an infrared spectrometer dangling from the brim. These are the three relevant hats that Jarred wears. It brings him profound joy that he can speak freely about drug checking results and findings with all of you. The amount of things to go right for drug checking to exist at a music festival should not be understated.

artists.name: The Quantum Forest: The New Science of Forest Hydrology
artists.description:
Workshop Description
In this workshop, filmmaker Dan Pierce and cartographer Nikita Wallia will take participants on a mindblowing journey into the complex systems of forests and watersheds. Beginning with the land where Bass Coast is situated and the waters where we all swim and play, we will follow the path of the Coldwater River up to its forested headwaters, many kilometres away. With Dan's stunning drone photography and Nikita's skills with maps, this will be a revealing aerial tour that will shift the audience's perspectives on where this vital water source originates from. 

Dan and Nikita will also break down the new science of forest hydrology, a cutting-edge probabilistic framework developed by Dr. Younes Alila out of his lab at UBC, which reveals the immense power of forests in mitigating extremes such as floods, landslides and droughts. Through stunning time-lapses of satellite imagery and forestry data, we will expose how decades of clearcut logging has degraded our most powerful protection from flood risk and drought risk in this province, contributing to devastating floods, including in Merritt in 2021. 

We will zoom out to reveal how loss of forest cover from wildfires and logging is setting us up for massive costs and liabilities from such disasters all over BC. But that doesn't have to be the end of the story. Dan and Nikita will then flip the script and present their case for why forest conservation and restoration is not only an ecological necessity, but an economic imperative. By conserving remaining primary forests and restoring forests that have already been degraded, we can protect our communities, strengthen our local economies, and put people to work in a generational project that will save billions of dollars in the long run.

Facilitator Biographies
Daniel Pierce is a journalist and documentary filmmaker. His film Trouble in the Headwaters explored how clearcutting contributed to the 2018 Grand Forks flood. His latest project, The Cutblock, is a YouTube channel exploring forest policy and reform in BC.

Nikita Wallia is a cartographer who uses mapping to visualise the climate and biodiversity crises. She specialises in GIS and forestry data and brings a deep knowledge of BC’s ecological transformation.

artists.name: Creative Neuroplasticity: Rewiring the Brain
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Discover how art-making literally changes your brain. In this engaging, hands-on workshop, art therapist Farrell Elliott explores the science of neuroplasticity, the brain’s ability to rewire and heal through creativity. Learn how visual art activates regions linked to emotion, memory, and resilience, and experience how creative expression can support mental health, identity, and growth. Participants will engage in an interactive art activity designed to awaken new neural pathways and deepen self-awareness. No art experience needed, just curiosity and an open mind.

Facilitator Biography
Farrell Elliott (they/them) is a Professional Art Therapist, Registered Therapeutic Counsellor, and Mindfulness Facilitator based in the Cowichan Valley, BC. Farrell specialises in neurodivergent and trauma-informed practice, integrating creative expression, neuroscience, and mindfulness to promote emotional regulation, resilience, and personal growth. With over 15 years of experience, Farrell’s work emphasises the connection between art-making, neuroplasticity, and holistic well-being, offering participants a grounded and evidence-based approach to self-discovery through creativity.

artists.name: Love and Freedom: Relationships Beyond Monogamy
artists.description:
Workshop Description
We are all wired for connection, but many of us aspire to relationship styles beyond monogamy, which is often the default. This workshop explores non-monogamous structures, including polyamory, “monogamish” setups, open relationships, and more.

Through a combination of insight, practical tools, and interactive exercises, participants will learn to navigate alternative relationships with clarity, compassion, and effective communication. You’ll leave with an understanding of different relationship types beyond monogamy, tips for having meaningful conversations, and strategies for cultivating relationships grounded in Love, Freedom, and Consent. This session challenges assumptions about what is possible in relationships while offering actionable guidance for real-world connection.

Facilitator Biographies
Megan Veaudry teaches Erotic Autonomy and is the founder of Non-Monogamy Coaching Vancouver. Drawing on relationship science and wisdom from the polyamorous community, she helps people question default relationship scripts and build connections rooted in choice. Megan supports people via 1:1 coaching, workshops, and group programs.

Aiofe Freeman-Cruz, PhD, is a psychologist based in Alberta who supports individuals and relationship groups, with a focus on marginalized identities and relational diversity. As a therapist, supervisor, and educator, Aiofe is passionate about accessible approaches as she blends self-discovery with practical tools. Drawing from systemic and postmodern approaches, she empowers individuals and groups as they seek authenticity and mental wellness.

artists.name: To Strike the Heart and Stay: Poetics for Integration
artists.description:
Workshop Description
“To Strike the Heart and Stay - Poetics for Integration” features a mix of spoken word poetry performance, discussion and generative writing prompts designed to foster a deeper understanding of the practice of poetry as a way of processing the world and exploring your place in it. This workshop is a consent-based space, designed to accommodate varying levels of experience and participation; show up to listen, write, or share, to whatever degree works for you.

Facilitator Biography
Marie Metaphor Specht is an acclaimed poet and performer, and the 6th Poet Laureate Emerita of Victoria, BC. With decades of experience as a mentor and workshop leader, she has nurtured countless writers across Canada. Known for her intimacy and honesty on stage, Marie believes in the power of stories shared and the bravery of beauty.

artists.name: Trick like a jellyfish, flow like the sea: Hula hoop workshop
artists.description:
Workshop Description
This year's workshop weaves together elements of many past Bass Coast themes, bringing back the hoop fun and tricks that worked well in the past and keeping the themes running. Starting with a Space journey, we’ll descend down to play like Wild Things, considering what the underwater Reflection would look like for our new hoop moves. You’ll learn tricks and combos that you can then take out into the festival and use to connect with new friends and other hoopers… or perhaps shine your new tricks out like a Prism and inspire someone else.
Even if you don’t hoop, come for the fun and giggles as we explore how the hoop connects us and how much fun it can be, Into the Deep Blue.

Bring your hoops, or use mine. We’ll end with the opportunity to try my 24 hoop slinky, a special event that will blow you away!

Facilitator Biography
Laura Loops is a passionate hula hooper whose mission is to inspire and support everyone to live their most Radiant Life and enjoy their hoops along the way. Laura has taught hoop classes for 10 years locally and abroad, leading her to facilitate engaging workshops that are fresh and exciting. While she sometimes still hits the stage, lately her focus has been on sharing the joy of hoops and the community it creates around her. If you see her on the dancefloor, come learn something!

artists.name: Friends of Dorothy: Queer & Trans Community Dance Jam
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Friends of Dorothy is a queer and trans-centred community dance workshop rooted in care, connection, and collective joy. Open to all bodies, abilities, and experience levels, this session is designed to be accessible, low-pressure, and welcoming.  No choreography or prior dance experience required. Participants are guided through gentle prompts, grounding practices, and playful movement exploration, with space to move in whatever way brings them joy. While open to everyone, the workshop intentionally centres queer and trans participants, prioritising consent, autonomy, and respect. Come as you are, connect with others, and move in a way that feels supportive to you.

Facilitator Biography
Nikita Rei (any pronouns) is a Black queer dancer, teacher, and community facilitator based in so-called Vancouver. With roots in multiple dance styles and a specialty in K-pop and Heels, Nikki’s work goes beyond choreography to honour the cultural, political, and historical contexts of movement and music. They are known for their joyful, encouraging facilitation style, creating spaces that prioritise play, consent, and self-trust. Nikki believes dance is for everyone and uses guided movement to help people connect with themselves and with each other.

Shayla Munn is a queer, autistic dance educator and the founder of Where We Land Movement Corp, with over 10 years of teaching experience. Their work centres safer, accessible spaces for gender- and sexually-diverse communities, as well as neurodivergent and disabled dancers. Shayla approaches movement as a tool for healing, joy, and collective care, with a deep respect for the social history of dance and its role in building community through art.

artists.name: Whose Genre? Rockism, Poptimism, and Authenticity
artists.description:
Workshop Description
What is authentic music when genre is not a fixed category, but a social construct negotiated by critics, marketers, and audiences? We look at the history of genre itself by exploring rockist discourses that configured rock as the most authentic genre, reconstructing it to be White and male, diverging from its roots in Black communities. We also discuss the poptimist reaction to rockism that sought to elevate other genres. 

Drawing on musical examples, reviews, and scholarship, we unpack genre and authenticity in music, looking at how rockist and poptimist discourses continue to impact our understanding of musical genres like EDM. I aim to foster a back-and-forth conversation so that participants can bring in their own experiences and leave with a perspective informed by their fellow ravers.

Facilitator Biography
Kolby Zinger-Harris is a PhD candidate in musicology at the University of Toronto, completing a collaborative specialisation in sexual diversity studies. His research, supported by an SSHRC Doctoral Fellowship, examines how musical production, cultural policy, and branding influence neoliberal subjectivity. 

Kolby’s work spans both popular and classical music, addressing how creators and audiences navigate political and economic structures. He also holds an MA in musicology and a BMus in composition, and works as a composer, arranger, and producer.

artists.name: Sound Bath: I’m a baby in the womb
artists.description:
Workshop Description
This workshop is a guided sound bath inspired by the experience of being a baby in the womb. The experience evokes the feeling of being held, floating, and gently supported, offering nervous system regulation through soothing, repetitive, and enveloping sound textures. Using low-frequency singing bowls, watery tones, rhythmic pulses resembling a heartbeat, and moments of near silence, the soundscape is slow, minimal, and deeply calming.

The intention of this sound bath is to reconnect participants with a sense of primordial safety and trust. By recreating womb-like sonic conditions, the session supports deep relaxation, emotional release, and a return to simplicity. Designed as a grounding counterpoint to the stimulation of a festival environment, participants are invited to soften, receive, and recalibrate through sound. No prior experience is required.

Facilitator Biography
Dali is the founder of Deseo Studio and has facilitated sound baths for over three years. Her regular offerings at Banyen Books and Sound, Coal Harbour Community Centre, and The Playground Studio focus on nervous system regulation, rest, and connection. Dali emphasises approachable themes and gentle guidance, welcoming both first-timers and experienced participants.

artists.name: Fuel the Magic: Nutrition for the Dance Floor and Recovery
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Our bodies are the vessels for the magic we experience at Bass Coast. To sustain that energy from the first set to the final sunrise, we need more than wellness quick fixes; we need evidence-based nutrition. Join Registered Dietitian Jill Worboys for a workshop on the science of festival self-care.

Explore how nutrition protects your brain, from serotonin pathways to electrolyte balance. Learn how to hydrate properly, maximise antioxidant intake, and fuel for recovery. Includes a hands-on Electrolyte Lab.

Facilitator Biography
Jill is a Registered Dietitian with 14 years of clinical and public health experience. Based in the Okanagan, she prioritises trauma-informed, weight-inclusive, and values-led care. She’s also a budding DJ and outdoor adventurer.

Her mission at Bass Coast: replace wellness grift with science-backed strategies that empower people to care for their physiology as an act of radical self-care.

artists.name: The Abomination Station
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Join Cait and Jordan for a crash course in hand-sewing with a delightful twist: Vegan Taxidermy. Participants will be guided in basic sewing techniques before dissecting and reassembling ethically sourced, once-loved stuffed animals into new, fantastical creations. Think: Octo-Bear, Racoon-Frog, Eagle-Lobster-Barbie.

All materials are provided. Come solo or with a friend, no biology degree required.

Facilitator Biographies
Cait (she/her) is a circus arts teacher and licensed clown.

Jordan (she/her) is a high school biology teacher and unlicensed clown.

Together, they bring a passion for teaching new skills, infusing function with whimsy, and encouraging the birth of new magical companions.

artists.name: Flow Slow, Dive Deep: Yang Yin Yoga
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Flow Slow, Dive Deep: Yang Yin Yoga. Dive into expansive movements to revitalise and restore your body. This intuitive and gentle movement practice will build the connection between body, mind, and breath. We will warm the body up, shake off the efforts of your day, and move through a calm yoga flow to limber up your muscles and joints. From there, we’ll dive into long, quiet stretches to open the body up and recharge you for the rest of your festival experience. Options will be given for any scale, whether you want to drive your body hard or just let it softly unfold. This safe but strong approach to yoga is for all levels, no experience required, with lots of variations for both the first timer and the seasoned yogi.

Facilitator Biography
Mix equal parts Breath, Curiosity, Strength, and Play, and you will have the recipe that Jeff enters into his teaching with. Seeing the body as our closest form of reality, yet also a source of constant mystery, movement can be a primary mode of embodiment and mindfulness, and this is what Jeff tries to bring to his skillfully crafted sequences. A full-time yoga and pilates teacher and personal trainer in Vancouver, BC, Jeff leads dynamic, well-rounded classes that help to unfold our inner strength and to cast light on our shadows. A firm believer that this practice is one that should be accessible and open to everybody, Jeff leads classes in an approachable language, with an emphasis on safe alignment, and an expansive knowledge of meeting the needs of practitioners of all levels.

artists.name: Booty Luv® Fitness
artists.description:
Workshop Description
BOOTY LUV® FITNESS is a bass-heavy, all-levels combo of FUN-ctional movement, yoga, non-impact cardio and booty breakdowns™ that take us deep into the brain/body connection. Bring a yoga mat and your friends to this festival fave that will get your hearts pumping and booties bumping. Be prepared to giggle and wiggle that delicious jelly!

Facilitator Biography
Heather Craig is an experienced trainer/instructor/raver/retreat leader based in Portland, OR. Her passion is helping people connect in POSITIVE ways to their bodies and each other. Her motto: FEEL GREAT in your body, DO BETTER in the world. Join her for in-person classes, CyberBooty™ On Demand and transformative retreats at www.bootyluvfitness.com.

artists.name: Queer Ice Cream Social
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Queerness is everywhere – especially at Bass Coast! Join us for a relaxed and inclusive social hour specifically curated for festival attendees who identify as a part of the 2SLGBTQIA+ community. Whether you are looking for new friendships, playful connections or just a place to be your queerest self, we look forward to curating a comfortable queer-forward space for all these moments to occur. Come as you are – connect at your own pace with zero expectations, in celebration of our collective queerness and community.

Facilitator Biographies
Galen (he/they) and Raysa (she/her) are a queer couple based in what is colonially known as Vancouver.

Galen is a trans‑masculine non‑binary artist and skilled barber, passionate about uplifting queer and transgender voices and creating opportunities for representation.

Raysa, originally from Brazil, has called Canada home for 10 years. Her experience as a queer immigrant has shaped her resilience and love of community‑building. By day, she works in youth mental health, bringing empathy and curiosity to all she does.

Together, they are dedicated to creating spaces that celebrate queer joy and connection.

artists.name: YOGA FLOW + LIVE INDIAN BEATS (INTO THE DEEP BLUE EDITION)
artists.description:
Workshop Description
It’s Saturday Morning. It’s time to wake up and flow to the Cabin Stage with yoga asana postures (with deep blue variations for all levels) by Sina Shalbaf, accompanied by live Indian sitar and warm mellow beats by Fred Ghatala. This inclusive high-vibe workshop has become a Bass Coast staple, refined over the years to help you tune in and limber up for an amazing start to your Saturday. Start with us, or join as the class is underway; you are always welcome. 

Facilitator Biography
Yoga Flow + Live Indian Beats is a Bass Coast creation for those who know the first on the mat catches the magic, and strong beginnings lead to luminous results. This workshop is designed by Sina Shalbaf (Honest Farms Retreats @ Salt Spring Island, DharmaTemple @ Vancouver) and Fred Ghatala (Jambhavan). Together, they have been weaving this yoga and sound experience for over nine years.

artists.name: Astro Moon Yin Yoga
artists.description:
Workshop Description
