In this Yin workshop, Brittany will guide you through a gentle, reflective exploration of current astrological influences and planetary movements, offering insight into how these energies can be consciously engaged with in daily life. The practice weaves together astrology, Yin Yoga, and visualisation, inviting participants to slow down and attune to the rhythms of both the cosmos and the body. Rooted in Yin Yoga’s connection to the meridian system, the class is informed by Brittany’s growing studies and interest in Chinese medicine, exploring how energy moves through the body in cycles, much like the planets above. Long-held postures will be accompanied by sacred poetry, intuitive astrological reflections, and guided imagery to support deeper softening and inner listening.

Participants are invited to bring a journal and pen, as well as anything else that supports grounding and presence. This offering is an invitation to rest, receive, and reflect, creating space for insight, integration, and alignment on both energetic and embodied levels.

Facilitator Biography
Brittany is a Vinyasa and Yin yoga teacher, mama of two, DJ (Rave Coach), Nutritionist, and astrology enthusiast. She currently resides on Salt Spring Island with her family, where she helps steward the land at Honest Farms, an eco-centre she co-created with her partner, Sina. With over a decade of teaching experience, Brittany has facilitated yoga, sound, and movement offerings throughout Vancouver, the Sea-to-Sky region and Vancouver Island. Her work is deeply influenced by cyclical wisdom, somatic awareness, and a growing devotion to Eastern medicine, as she prepares to continue her studies in Chinese medicine. One of Brittany’s greatest passions is leading new and full moon circles and sharing the wisdom of astrology as a tool for reflection, intention, and personal transformation. In her gatherings, she works with lunar energy to support manifestation and reconnection to one’s inner compass. You can expect to leave her offerings feeling grounded, uplifted, and inspired; open to what is unfolding and excited for what lies ahead.

artists.name: Unity Partner Massage
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Unity Partner Massage is a reciprocal exchange of energy and hands-on healing with a partner, friend, or perfect stranger. The session will start with grounding breathwork and simple Qi Gong exercises to enhance our sensitivities to the energy of the earth and the heavens, and to establish energetic and physical boundaries and consent. We will progress through a simple and accessible therapeutic massage where both partners will give and receive. Finishing with a collective exercise, we will join our voices together in chant, opening a portal to the sky!

Facilitator Biography
Susan is a skilled facilitator, bodyworker, and energy healer passionate about bringing people together through practices that awaken body, mind, and spirit. She offers yoga retreats, partner bodywork training, and is also a skilled herbalist and astrologer. Ask her about personal destiny readings or retreats to the Gulf Islands!

artists.name: ROOTS FLOW
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Roots Flow is a playful, music-centred practice that blends yoga, movement, somatic release, sound and meditation. Guided by Rae Roots, the workshop weaves postures, breath, moments of stillness, and partner exercises. A fun way to ground, restore and connect.
Sound supports the experience, sometimes as a subtle background while exploring complex movement, and at other times encouraging dance, vocal release, and expression. Music draws from Jamaica, Africa, the UK, Egypt, and beyond, spanning dub, reggae, breaks, jungle, Afro-house, ancestral rhythms, and meditative sounds.

Acknowledging the roots of yoga in India and drawing from wisdom gained through years of practice and study with senior yoga teachers, Rae shares both guided sequences and leaves space to explore your own way of moving. This workshop balances energy from root to crown.

Facilitator Biography
RAE ROOTS (E-RYT 500) with over 1000 hours of yoga teacher training, is a dedicated yoga practitioner, teacher, DJ, and radio show host with a background in biomedical sciences. Founder of the neighbourhood yoga shala in East Vancouver (@raerootsyoga) — a down-to-earth space rooted in care and respect for yoga’s origins, Rae sees yoga as a path to the heart, to connect wholly to our nature and to live with greater clarity and joy. She teaches in a way that is both informative and welcoming, with an approach that is playful and attentive, keeping practice accessible while honouring the profound depth of yoga as a spiritual practice.

artists.name: Yin and Sound
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Take a little pause and slow down with a yin and sound journey. Combining the gentle, meditative practice of Yin Yoga with the therapeutic sounds of instruments such as singing bowls, vocals, chimes, and more, this fusion creates an immersive experience to relax the body and calm the mind. 

Yin is a powerful practice to release stuck tension from the fascia, the connective tissues of the body, and restore range of motion. And sound is like a gentle massage for the places where our hands can't reach. This is a perfect offering to complement the bass coast experience, allowing you to drop in and take a pause in between all the fun.

Facilitator Biography
Gin Perry is a yoga teacher, sound practitioner, and cultural explorer devoted to bridging ancient wisdom with modern awareness. Drawing from years of study in Yoga philosophy, Nada Yoga, and Shamanic traditions, her work weaves breath, movement, and vibration into an embodied journey of presence and connection.

Guardian of Shala Yoga in Squamish, BC, she guides students toward stillness through practices that harmonise body and sound: pranayama and asana, immersive soundscapes and voice journeys.

Having studied with teachers across India, North and South America, she brings a cross-cultural sensitivity and deep respect for lineage to her offerings. At the heart of her teaching is the belief that through sound and silence, we remember our original harmony, with ourselves and with the Earth.

artists.name: Embodied Flow Yoga: Fire Horse Rising
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Join Amy Heasman for a 75-minute Embodied Flow Yoga experience inspired by the bold, expansive energy of the Year of the Fire Horse. 

This dynamic class invites you to embody, align, and ground into your physical, emotional, and energetic bodies through a fusion of hatha yoga, somatic movement, breathwork, vocal release, and mindful awareness. Together we’ll explore the balance between strength and softness, fluidity and stillness, while awakening inner vitality and personal freedom.

Accessible for all levels, with modifications offered. Come ignite your inner fire, move with intention, and reconnect with your natural flow.

Facilitator Biography
Amy Heasman is a 300-hour Yoga Teacher, vocalist/songwriter, and the Founder & Executive Director of the Kootenay Wellness Festival. Born in East London, UK, she now resides in the Kootenays, BC, where she brings over 15 years of experience creating embodied, music-infused wellness spaces.

Amy’s work is rooted in humility, gratitude, and deep respect for yogic lineages. She weaves movement, breath, sound, and vocal vibration into her offerings to support healing, self-awareness, and authentic expression. As a festival director, she works in relationship with the Autonomous Sinixt to ensure her offerings honour the land and actively avoid cultural appropriation, fostering inclusive and culturally respectful community spaces.

artists.name: KLN
artists.description: Kailyn, known as KLN, is a Whistler-based DJ and event curator active since 2019, with roots in the underground rave scene and a strong focus on community-led events. As a core member of Community Sound System and co-founder of Babe Rave, she’s helped produce high-energy parties and fundraisers across Whistler, including appearances at Spacedaze Festival. Her sound spans groove-led, funk and house selections for daytime settings to deeper, driving techno for late-night raves, delivering versatile, energetic sets that connect with the crowd.

artists.name: Sn00ze
artists.description:
sn00ze is a “Vancouver” based Filipino DJ. Inspired by hip hop, soul, funk, and jazz, sn00ze builds the pillars of his musical intrigue which continue to shape the sounds he mixes today. That foundation comes through in his selections of jungle, footwork, juke, jersey club, Miami bass, and ghettotech. sn00ze blends all of these influences into sets that feel familiar and new at the same time, inviting listeners to move, laugh, and feel on the dancefloor.

He is a core member of BDrive, a collective that prides itself on showcasing underground talent and local artistry. sn00ze has shared bills with DJ Assault, DJ Swisha, Khadija Al Hanafi, and DazeGxd.


sn00ze currently hosts Mixed Signals on BSIDE Radio, a monthly non-profit radio show live streamed every first Thursday.

artists.name: CATaria
artists.description: CATaria is a Colombian DJ and curator based in Vancouver, known for her precision and groove-driven sound. Since 2018, her journey has taken her through iconic clubs and festivals in Colombia and now to the most renowned techno venues in Vancouver. Blending groove, raw and hypnotic rhythms, with strong house elements, she crafts immersive, high-energy sets with a clear narrative. Her ability to shape the crowd and create unforgettable atmospheres, along with her respect for underground culture, has earned her recognition, sharing stages with renowned artists such as Adriana Lopez, Nastia, Alarico, and OTTA, among others. A Vantek resident, she commands the dancefloor, where groove becomes ritual and movement becomes connection.

artists.name: Elemental Fusion: Qigong & Bass Bath
artists.description:
Workshop Description
Embark on a transformative journey through the five elements: earth, water, fire, air, and ether, connecting them to the chakras and your innate energy. This dynamic movement workshop integrates the practices of tantra, Qigong, and meditation, with Science and sound healing to awaken your senses and realign your mind, body, and spirit. Guided by indigenous-rooted opening and closing ceremonies, participants will explore the interplay of movement, energy, and sound, embracing ancient wisdom and modern practices for deep connection and renewal. Step into a sacred space with Hailey and Joshua, full of discovery, flow, and connection.

Facilitator Biographies
Hailey Hamilton is a Métis Medicine Woman of Treaty Six who embodies her culture through singing, drumming, and dance in ritual and ceremony. Hailey is a Registered Yoga Instructor (RYT 585), Registered Holistic Nutritionist, and Certified Birth Doula with specialised training in both TCM and Ayurveda. As a Holistic Mentor, Hailey empowers others by guiding them back home to their innate joy, in reverence for the land on which we thrive. 

Joshua Holt is a certified Tantra Teacher, Professional Mechanical Engineer, and accomplished Producer/DJ, utilising his scientific and musical backgrounds to share the wisdom of the Tantra practice. Teaching workshops and DJing at festivals around the world, Josh focuses on breaking down the barrier between Science and Spirituality to develop a stronger connection between the mind, body, and soul.

artists.name: Lil’ Guy and Jungle Kat
artists.description:
Performances
Friday | 8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Slowtempo Stage hosting The Deep Sea Cabaret
Sunday | 8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Slowtempo Stage hosting The Deep Sea Cabaret

Performer Biographies
Lil’ Guy, Hannah Shaka (she/her) is a Canadian clown, puppeteer, musician, actor and teaching artist. She has over 10 years experience as a performer and teaching artist, and holds an MFA in Ensemble Based Physical Theatre. She has performed and led workshops at festivals across the US, and performs regularly in Los Angeles. Whether performing an original puppet musical, or creating an immersive character for a special event, Hannah always brings fully-transformed and hysterical characters that invite you to play joyously. At the core of her work, Hannah asks: How can the clown use connection, play and joy to make the world a better place?

Jungle Kat is a neo-burlesque performer, dancer, and instructor from Vancouver, BC. From London to Las Vegas, she has graced some of the most prestigious, internationally-acclaimed burlesque and festival stages. Considering herself a true collaborator, she has worked with numerous troupes to create special experiences for many audiences, including Pin-Up Posse, La Maison Lust, Lost Girls Burlesque, and Screaming Chicken Theatrical Society.

artists.name: Chérie Coquette
artists.description:
Performance Statement
Chérie Coquette’s burlesque performance blends contemporary choreography, theatricality, and playful cabaret. Sultry, humorous, and electrifying, her show celebrates joy, confidence, and self-expression, leaving audiences charmed and captivated.
 
Performer Biography
Originally from Montreal, Chérie Coquette is a burlesque performer and trained contemporary choreographer in Whitehorse, Yukon. Known for her playful charm, fearless creativity, and magnetic presence, she turns every performance into a dazzling mix of movement, humour, and audience connection.

artists.name: The Lucid Fairy
artists.description:
Performances
Friday | 8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Slowtempo Stage during The Deep Sea Cabaret
Sunday | 8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Slowtempo Stage during The Deep Sea Cabaret

Performer Biography
The Lucid Fairy is a Vancouver-based electro-pop drag artist delivering high-energy, concert-style performances featuring original music alongside electrifying covers. Her precision movement and angelic vocals have captivated audiences locally and internationally, with appearances at major music festivals and Pride celebrations as both host and entertainer.

Lucid’s mission is to inspire radical self-expression, self-love, community, and harmony.

artists.name: Tooth and Nail
artists.description:
Performer Biographies
Tooth and Nail is a collaborative movement project between Emily Long (HushHush) and Alex Philcox (Kidsacred). This duo studies love, death, sex, agony and bliss, catharsis, impulse, and spirit through improv movement, contact, contemporary dance, clown/character work, acrobatics, butoh, and new forms. Their work is equally vulnerable as imaginative, creating space for raw humanity within a haunting fantasy.

artists.name: i Love Hoola Hoops Sisters
artists.description:
Performer Biographies
Actual sister’s, Dixie Honey and Celeste with I Love Hula Hoops have been performing together for over 17 years, specialising in multi-hoop flow art . Speeding up and slowing down the movement one hoop at a time.

artists.name: Mystic Flow
artists.description:
Performance Statement
Daughter of the Deep is a mystical, enchanting performance, drawing you into the hidden depths of your soul. Through sensual, electric movement, Mystic Flow pulls you out of your mind and into your body.
 
Performer Biography
Mystic Flow is a multidisciplinary movement artist with over 15 years of training and performance experience. A natural-born performer, she captivates audiences through ethereal presence, fluidity, and expressive movement.

artists.name: Punk.Pose.WH/AACK!
artists.description:
Performance Statement
Punk.Pose.Wh/aack! returns with a dynamic exploration of Punking and Wh/aacking dance. Rooted in Queer liberation and expression, Wh/aacking brings the music to life by striking, posing, and “punking” the beat. Directed by Nikki Mae, this team brings together a group of Vancouver street and club dancers who each bring their own approach to movement, unified by Wh/aacking, to highlight sounds of the underground.

Performer Biography
Nikki Mae (She/Her) is a movement artist based in Vancouver. Having explored many genres, her current focus is on Whacking/Punking dance. She is the founding member of Omnika In Motion and has been performing at festivals and underground events since 2013 inspiring shared moments of connection and storytelling through movement.

artists.name: Queen Kong
artists.description:
Performances
Sunday | 8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Slowtempo Stage during The Deep Sea Cabaret

Performance Biography
With over a decade of pole and drag performance experience, Queen Kong brings high octane performances with the blend of sultry, seductive and sensational aesthetics and energy. Hailing from Hong Kong she’s the angel on the streets demoness in the sheets and the beast from the East.

artists.name: Dance Anonymous
artists.description:
Performance Statement
This piece traces the current from everyday life into the deep blue of the rave. It follows the quiet rituals we carry, then submerges them into collective release. Movement shifts like water- fluid, tidal, immersive. Local designers and thrifted pieces flow through the work, grounded in community, while a soundscape rooted in Bass Coast culture pulls us deeper into our own ocean.

Performer Biography
Dance Anonymous is a collective of movers, each excelling in different styles, who come together to connect through movement. On stage or the dancefloor, we let go of labels and focus on presence, improvisation, and creation. We don’t always define what we’re doing — we just dance, explore, and build connection through the shared act of movement.

artists.name: shelajit
artists.description:
Performer Biography
Shelajit is a dynamic performer and multidisciplinary artist blending 25 years of training in contemporary dance, hip hop, and classical styles with the precision and power of martial arts weaponry and avant garde fashion. Rooted in Chinese ancestry and inspired by texture and experimentation they create captivating and attuned performances. With a career spanning hundreds of live stage performances, Shelajit has also contributed their artistry to numerous music videos in recent years. As a music producer and DJ, Shelajit’s intimate connection to sound adds an unparalleled depth and rhythm to their performances, creating a holistic sensory experience.

artists.name: Dollhaus
artists.description:
Performance Statement
This piece traces the current from everyday life into the deep blue of the rave. It follows the quiet rituals we carry, then submerges them into collective release. Movement shifts like water- fluid, tidal, immersive. Local designers and thrifted pieces flow through the work, grounded in community, while a soundscape rooted in Bass Coast culture pulls us deeper into our own ocean.

Performer Biography
Dance Anonymous is a collective of movers, each excelling in different styles, who come together to connect through movement. On stage or the dancefloor, we let go of labels and focus on presence, improvisation, and creation. We don’t always define what we’re doing — we just dance, explore, and build connection through the shared act of movement.

artists.name: Pearly Twirls
artists.description:
Performer Biography
Pearly Twirls is a well versed freestyle and gogo dancer known for her magnetic stage presence and captivating movements that can't help but turn heads. With 12 years of freestyle dance experience, 10 years attending festivals, years of training in various styles, and 3 years of teaching dance to others Pearl has built a wide range of expertise that is reflected in her dance style as she blends together many different styles of dance in her performances to create a distinctive experience that is one of a kind, entertaining and high energy to keep crowds engaged.

artists.name: The FloWarrior
artists.description:
Performance Statement
Having experienced it first hand, The FloWarrior is a strong believer in the power of art to transform lives for the better.
Given the gift of inspiration as a teenager, it changed the course of his life.
His mission is to pay forward and share this gift of inspiration with the world through his work performing, teaching, and motivational speaking.
His performance is an expression of his passion for this art form, which he utilises to amaze, inspire, and empower audiences of all ages.
Having invested intentionally into exploring and developing new artistic practices, he seeks to push the limits of what is possible through movement art and object manipulation.
By doing so, he hopes to create a positive impact in the world by bringing joy and wonder to people's lives, and empowering and inspiring them with his story.

Performer Biography 
Fusing his passion for dance, martial arts and music, The FloWarrior has created his own unique brand of ‘Martial Artistry’ through 20 years of training and dedication to his craft.
 
A true professional with over a decade of experience performing and teaching, The FloWarrior's lifelong love of Asian culture, philosophy, and martial arts is evident in every aspect of his performance and style.
 
A world renowned innovator in a number of unique disciplines including Folding Fans, Rice Hats, Lightsabers, Fire Katanas, and The Force Leash; he continues to push the limits of what is possible through movement art and object manipulation.

artists.name: King Dom
artists.description:
Performer Biography
A prolific performer throughout western Canada’s burlesque and drag communities, King Dom trained in New York City specialising in contemporary and ballet before moving to London, England, where his professional dance career really took off. King Dom has danced his way around the globe as a recurring dancer on various cruise ships and touring productions. King Dom is a signed dancer with DaCosta Talent Management, Canada’s elite dance agency for film and tv and if you look close you can find him dancing in many scenes of your favourite shows. Recently, King Dom returned to his hometown of Edmonton where he has been teaching locally and performing with the House of Hush Burlesque Company.

artists.name: Soaring Stilt Dance
artists.description:
Performance Statement
Soaring Stilt Dance, are driven by a passion for innovation and a commitment to reshaping the narrative of Stilt Performance. With every act, they aim to not only captivate audiences but also inspire change within the circus industry. There stilts become more than just a means of elevation; they are a symbol of breaking free from conventional norms and soaring to new creative heights.
 
They are not only pushing the boundaries of performance but also about fostering collaborative creativity with local costume designers. They believe in the power of innovation and sustainability, and they have embarked on a journey to craft costumes that not only dazzle on stage but also prioritise environmental responsibility.
 

Performer Biographies
Soaring Stilt Dance, is an awe-inspiring duo residing on the unceded and illegally occupied territories of the Squamish, Tsleil-Waututh and Musqueam Nation. They aim to defy gravity and elevate entertainment to unparalleled heights! Through a mesmerising fusion of soft acrobatics and enchanting dance, this duo is poised to captivate audiences locally and internationally. With more than a decade of experience in circus, dance, and theatre performance, Lola Loops & Bella Bizarre are thrilled to collectively invite you into their captivating world.

artists.name: Celestial Sirens
artists.description:
Performance Statement
After soaring through the skies; the Sirens return to their celestial roots within the deep blue of the sea, calling out their song for all to wish upon a star and follow their dreams. A collective of multi-media and multi-faceted artists with a range of experiences within the performance arts. Their passions and devotions lay in various realms, curating atmospheric creations that enthrall and encaptivate audiences through the creation of custom garments, and intentional movements designed to cast spells upon the surroundings.
 

Performer Biographies
Shae aka Siren is no stranger to the crowd; more so a slave to the sound, and the dance floor is her holy ground. An experienced performer and multimedia artist from the Victoria region, she amalgamates her crafts into entrancing displays of expression designed to enthrall audiences. While balancing existence in the physical realm, they flow through the freedom of physical expression, casting spells into soil through intentional manifestation and movement.
 

Heather/Lilah Lavande is a multi-faceted performer and artist whose signature slow-flow style weaves contemporary dance, pole, hoop, and silk fans into captivating and atmospheric performances. Her work is shaped by formal training in Performing and Visual Arts and professional experience at Disney World. Beyond the stage, she also creates as a fiber artist and DJ, bringing the same fluid creativity to every medium.
 

Wandering Artists: Margaux & Keven - partners in life and on stage - infuse moments with intensity, raw emotions and movement, pushing boundaries to create stunning and theatrical visual stories. As Wandering Artists, they embody their namesake, curating shows abroad fusing inspirations from diverse cultures and art forms. Their story is one of love, connection and exploration. An ode to the power of dark creativity and the beauty of wandering.
 

Sabine/Velvet Nymph is gulf island grown multidisciplinary performer that seeks to transport you to another realm. Whether through burlesque, dance, pole, hoop, feather or silk fans; she invites you to a place where a willingness to give into whimsy, allows access to forgotten magic. 
 

Corrie-Lee/Chimaera and Chrysalis is a queer multidisciplinary artist whose work spans sculpture, costume design, and dance. With a deep rooted foundation in both ballet and contemporary dance, they weave movement and material into enchanting experiences. Drawing inspiration from the natural world and what lies beyond the veil, they craft their pieces using sustainably sourced animal bones and a rich tapestry of found materials. Through their haunting sculptures, artistic endeavors and performances, they invite viewers to embrace their own mortality and discover the magic that lies within the ephemeral nature of existence.
